Man, GMX has some real slot machine combo routing, but the biggest problem is Noma. Her on field effect is absolutely insane, especially since she can summon a 4-5 dino COMPREX with ease, but pure GMX genuinely has no way to summon her with any consistency. The only answer I could come up with was sending her to grave with Mitsurugi Ritual, then reviving her off whatever loose revive is left. So you've got ALLOS, Kimridge stack > VELOX, Cross Sheep, Charmer Quartet, and Selandea to somehow get her onto the field.

GMX bridges into Mitsurugi with Archosaur (pop ALLOS/VELOX), and Mitsurugi bridges into GMX with Zombie Vampire. 12 GMX-sided starters, 4 Mitsurugi-sided ones, so consistency's actually ok, but sadly there's only enough space for 12 non-engine. Could maybe argue for cutting the GMX Trap, quickplay, or both - the routing frequently won't let you search either of them.

Turbo Obelisk into the grave and pound with The Breaking-Ruined God. you do this by turboing into "Branded Fusion", dumping Fallen of Albaz + Obelisk to fuse into Brigrand. Mitsurugi is to bait hand-traps and/or win-more with consistent board presence. Also when tributed for Obelisk and by Obelisk's effect, they trigger Mitsurugi's effects. Other win-more are Soul Energy Maxx!!, you can dump it into the grave to use it's graveyard effect for offense on your turn, or for defense on oppotnents turn (if you have enough tributes to do so). It's a one-trick pony, weak to hand-traps/distruptions, but Mitsurugi is to help you have some presence with Branded for board-breaking. Ecclesia is not in the main deck, but can be included if you want to make Dogma into a tower.  

If going second, you can add in 2 Droll, 1 Super Poly, 3 Fuwalos in exchage/take out: 1 Aramasa, 2 Prayers, 1 Kitt, 1 Cartesia, 1 Mercourier

deck_excerpt: Ashened and Dinomorphia come together with some power and using your opponents monsters as fusion material because sharing is caring :)
deck_description:
Ashened with Dinomorphia to bully your opponents field zone and get free special summons off Veidos the Eruption Dragon of Extinction by activating it to you opponents field zone. Then you can use the Dinomorphia cards to get even more summons off on your opponents turn, because Sharing is caring :) Full Combo write up to come soon but for now the basics are as follows:

If Veidos and Obsidim are not in hand. 

  1. Priestess of the Ashened City ----> Search Inferno of the Ashened 
  2. Activate Inferno of the Ashened placing Obsidim, the Ashened City into your opponents field zone 
  3. With Obsidim, the Ashened City now in play you can Special Summon any of the Ashened cards that are in your hand 
  4. Because most of the Ashened cards are level 4 your can XYZ Summon into Infernal Flame Banshee to search Veidos the Eruption Dragon of Extinction 
  5. Use Veidos the Eruption Dragon of Extinction effect to destroy Obsidim, the Ashened City in your opponents field then set Ashened to Endlessness from deck
  6. Use Obsidim, the Ashened City destroy effect to special summon King of the Ashened City 
  7. Activate King of the Ashened City to special summon any Ashened from the deck since your opponent controls a monster with 2800 ATK Hero of the Ashened City is a solid choice but if you have room or need the removal Spearhead of the Ashened City can XYZ summon into Evilswarm Exciton Knight, Tornado Dragon or Number 60: Dugares the Timeless 
  8. Use Ashened to Endlessness during opponents Main phase to fuse away Veidos the Eruption Dragon of Extinction and all your Pryo monsters you control to make Veidos the Dragon of Endless Darkness which will give you at minimum a 4500 ATK Veidos the Dragon of Endless Darkness which can not be destroyed by card effects and can not be targeted by monster effects

Placement: Top 8

The Ritual Engine & Board Breaking

Skull Archfiend functions as our primary, generic Ritual searcher. Because our Ritual Spell, Mitsurugi Mirror, explicitly lists the Ritual Monster's name, we can use Skull Archfiend to easily fetch Habakiri from the deck. Alternatively, if we already have our main combo pieces, we can search for Black Chaos Magician, giving us a highly effective tool for breaking established enemy boards.

 

Accessing Mind Shuffle

Consistency is key, and we have two dedicated routes to cheat Mind Shuffle directly out of the deck: it can be brought out using the effects of either Black Chaos the Ultimate Magical Swordsman or Gurifoh.

 

Synchro Extenders & Graveyard Setup

Fallen of the White Dragon serves as a vital combo piece, opening up two distinct Synchro pathways depending on the game state:

 

The Resonator Line: We can Quick Synchro into The Crimson King, which allows us to search for Crimson Call. A seamlessly bridge into getting Gurifoh online.

The Swordsoul / Dogmatika Line: Alternatively, we can Synchro Summon The Three Brave Swordsouls to fetch Dogmatika Lawbringer. Lawbringer then acts as our Extra Deck dumping mechanism, sending Herald of the Arc Light straight to the graveyard. Herald’s graveyard effect immediately triggers, netting us a free search for Habakiri to complete the setup.

deck_excerpt: Fiend, Monarch, Vanity, Majesty, Solidarity, Stun, Tower,
deck_description:
A full Fiend deck taking advantage of Solidarity atk booast, all the while having great draw power via a full monarch consistent protection as Majesty of Vanity lock out your opponent.

Utilizing the Archfiend engine Beast of Talwar is not just a great opening special summon, he protects himself too.

The Highness Archfiend also a great starter allowing you to grab Eccentric or Royal Archfiend.

Royal Archfiend is exact monarch stats with great synergy for all Monarch spells. His discard and banish effects assist too, the extra normal summon helps alot too.

Eccentric is great for whatever you need her to be either backrow or monster removal.

Spirit Illusion works great when equiped to Majesty with the additional tokens having the same stats and getting further powered with solidarity.

Seal of Oricahlcous works awesome as most of the monsters are 2400 atk so a lock becomes live if all your monsters the same attack.

Extra Deck is all Fiends aswell too, if needed for situations. Aeriel Eater can join the field off the tokens and fusion via the equip spell Spirir Illusion. We do not rely on the extra deck for any of our win conditions or boss monster, hence the draw power from Pot of Extravagence. Careful as pantheism plays not allowed in that turn where we use Pot. We make that decission at the start of Main Phase 1 depending on what else is in your hand.

Side deck all the best continous stun traps that can slow us down but can totally shut down certain match ups, but most of them being a 1 of makes it less reliable.

Royal decree to stop trap based decks, Chaos Hunter is great to stop banish based decks and she still does not mess up Solidarity and same with Lava Golem being Fiend.

If your opponent runs field cards then set rotation into Magic Mid-Breaker field is a great way to lock them out of using there own fields. Chicken Game works well if your opponent plays higher aggressive or alternate win condition decks.

Pure dark dog shi magician starter deck. I Recommend playing against blue-eyes starter deck or anthing similar and/or below this decks tier range.

Sky Striker deck for Yu-Gi-Oh! Legacy of the Duelist: Link Evolution. (Story & IA only)

General strat:

1. Setup: Pot of Greed, Graceful Charity to draw Engage! and send 3 spells to the GY.
2. Pressuring: Widow Anchor, Afterburners! to destroy or steal IA's monster. Thanks to Multirole they'll come back on your field.
3. Ending: Borrelsword Dragon.

Main Combo (you must have 2+ spells in you GY):

1. Activate Engage!: search your deck for Hornet Drones (you can draw 1 card if you have 3 spells in you GY)
2. Activate Hornet Drones and use the token to Link summon Kagari
3. Use Kagari's effect to get Engage! and activate it to search Multirole (or Widow Anchor in case you need defense). Draw a card.
4. Activate Multirole to send to your GY any useless card.
5. Usa Kagari to Link summon Shizuku.
6. During your end phase Shizuku will let you search a spell in your deck and Multirole will let you reposition all of your spells activated this turn.

OTK Combo:

1. Set your field using Widow Anchor to steal one IA's monster. Use Hornet Drones to summon a token.
2. Use Shizuku, the stolen monster and the token to Link summon Borrelsword Dragon.

TIPS:
Use Heavy Storm as your first move.
Don't be scared to use Graceful Charity, Sky Striker's spells are a resourse when in your GY (also you can get them back with Kagari).

Usage:


Dark World: Engine for sacred beast's discard cost effect
Sacred Beasts: Main Discard Engine, Material for xyz and the fusion

Guide:

Simple, each sacred beast of the 4 "except the creator" adds a card and discards a card, which triggers dark world, which rebuilds hand and summons a monster, also sacred beasts has draw engine (paradise), and can use rebuilt hands and extra monsters for extra summons
you can use hamon to draw, or raviel to add creator or uriel  to add trap to fuse and negate
or just keep summoning and xyz or Fuse or whatever, you also will really need the fusion summon dark world accession both for sacred and dark world, you can use sacred hamon or creator to go into colossus and you can summon colossus up to 3 times that way, which can also be a material for zombiestein or vampire which is an extender.



what's so good about this deck? 


>Unaffected by hand traps "Like Mermails", if you can summon fusion grapha at the beginning it will be even better (your only enemy is droll that now is matched by koa'ki, and the other one is shifter which isnt being used anymore)
>Silvva can shuffle during opponent's turn bec grapha fusion, or destroy cards using or grapha.
>Sacred Fusion easy 3 negates alone, can be resummoned with a bao link 4 for additional 3 negates total 6.
>can easily break boards, that's why I don't run handtraps, and don't try to add hand traps.
>can bypass many field negates if played correctly or with a good hand, I mean bad plays always suck no matter the deck right?
>can make a really good field going first, like...a really good field
>You can use zombiestein + any card really to negate droplet
>dark world's quick play spell allows for a second fusion of Sacred beasts, which can use "ANY" level 10 monsters that cannot be normal summoned, meaning it includes extra deck monsters and you can use monsters with the same name for the fusion.

deck_description:
I wanted to create the ultimate deck that Judai/Jaden Yuki could have access to by combining cards from his different appearances.

This deck contains cards Judai uses in the 4th season of GX anime and in the Bonds Beyond Time movie. I opted to leave out some of the generic cards that Judai uses, as I don't feel like they bring anything important to the deck and I try to keep all my anime decks at max 45 cards.

Sideboard is also used as an extra space for all the fusion monsters Judai runs. As the deck is meant for only anime duels, having a 26 card fusion deck should not be a problem, but if you want to run only 15, choose the ones that you like the best! I have my suggestions for the 15 in the fusion deck (Note: Neos Knight would be Neos Wiseman if Neos Wiseman was anime accurate fusion monster).

All the cards are meant to be played with their anime accurate effects.

If anyone has any feedback or suggestions, I would be very happy to hear them!

deck_excerpt: For the love of the game: bringing fun back to the battlefield
The new queens of Masterduel ladder has arrived, and they are the Elfnotes!
deck_description:
Elfnotes have finally arrived with the selection pack "Song of Divinity" to take centre stage on Masterduel ladder! These elven songstresses are the newest flavour of "midrange handtrap" style strategy, and it does it's job extremely well! The deck is fairly consistent with 14 one-card starters, and they feature the gameplan of various control effects (bounce monster, bounce spell/trap, handloop random card). These effects are done as they switch to the central monster zone - which is thematically really cool because these elves are effectively showing off their concert performance!

A lot of generic synchro boss monsters are accessible to these ladies, since they can easily make synchro 10s like Baronne or level 8s like Crystal Wing. They can also make a generic synchro 5 known as "Armades, Keeper of the Boundaries," which coincidentally is a light fiend, allowing access to the Fiendsmith cards! 

Finally, Super Polymerization is included in this deck to hardcounter Kewl Tunes, and also since it's generically powerful this format, often stopping entire turns or breaking apart powerful boards. 

Playing the strategy in Masterduel for the first time, my personal experience on ladder with Elfnote Fiendsmith is that the deck is powerful, but fragile. It can easily get interrupted as it combos, it's susceptible to Mulcharies/Droll and sometimes even low-impact handtraps. However, if it fully combos off, you're basically winning with a near unbreakable board and a ton of follow-up.

I'm eagerly looking forward to their future power-ups to propel these musical mavens to tier 1 status! 

GLHF Duelists! 

Want to see the deck in action? Scroll to the very bottom for the Youtube video!

deck_excerpt: Guide added. Other guides to come.
deck_description:
The Main Combo

You begin by summoning Primoa which then searches Happiness, then you use the search from that to add Gracia and Coolia (or if you can summon more cards and want the extra body, go for Cutea). Gracia, when summoned, then searches for Elegance and you'll use that to search two pendulum scales. If you searched Coolia earlier, you can place her in the Extra Deck to cover the card's cost. Don't worry, though, you'll Pendulum Summon her soon. Regardless, I usually search Beautia and Solfegia for a nice 2-9 scale. Link cards away, pendulum them back, do whatever you want. Everything after this depends on your starting hand.

Melodious and Fusions

I tried adding all of the good Melodious monsters as well as the ones that can special summon themselves. If you can get out two, that's enough for their Link monster, Bloom Harmonist. You can use her to bring out Aria and Elegy for protection, or you can summon cards that enable fusions. Tamtam is a very funny target since she adds Polymerization from your deck/GY to your hand and is not once per turn, should you find ways to keep bringing her out. Soprano has similar utility as well. With all of these cards that can fuse, I'll draw some attention to Bacha, who can keep things moving. She is incredibly easy to summon and can be used as a Fusion material or even for a Link summon. No matter how you do it, you're sure to get a few boss monsters in play.

Other Standout Cards

Dawn Angel Kambi is a great card due to being so easy to summon and in a pinch, she can search for Cutea. Sakitama also exists to make summoning easier, and it especially helps given that Bloom Harmonist locks you into special summoning only Melodious monsters. Since you'll be fusing a lot, Cross-Sheep will be really good for it's revival effect. I also like Mutiny in the Sky since it can recycle all of your Melodious cards should you use it to summon Bloom Prima. Refrain the Melodious Songstress can search for a Melodious card, so pick one with a special summon effect, and with those two, you can Link Summon Bloom Harmonist, who was mentioned earlier.

Negation

The main problem this deck has is dealing with negation. It can make plays and spit out a decent board, but it has a lot of unfortunate choke points that I have not been able to remedy. I felt like I did the best I possibly could, considering how clunky these archetypes are with only 1-ofs, but perhaps there may be other options that I haven't discovered. I know at one point, I even considered Performapal Ladyange as a deck master, which would have enabled access to Performapal and Odd-Eyes support. Still, it's more than fine in a casual pod.

 

Credit: Radiant Horns

deck_excerpt: AFTER ALL THESE YEARS. FINALLY, I HAVE THEM ALL!
deck_description:
The new Morganite cards are stupidly good thanks to Morgana the Witch of Eyes domain (DARK and/or Spellcaster). With her piloting you can start with Succumbing-Song Morganite and Guilt-Gripping Morganite. With this setup I have this effects up and running:

  • Normal summon level 5+ without tributes.
  • Activate Spell and Traps without paying LP.
  • Double attack on monsters.
  • Double damage on monsters.
  • No monster effects in hand.

I only need to summon Morgana again to get Time-Tearing Morganite and gain:

  • Two cards on each draw phase.
  • Two Normal summons on each turn.

Why I chose Earthbounds: 

  • Good ATK and DEF stats.
  • Immortals have powerful battle effects. They prevent being attacked and can attack directly. Morgana's third effect with one these guys attacking can take out someone from the game really quick.
  • Prisoners can set up synchro and fusion Servants to have interactions on the opponent's turn.

I considered this archetypes before picking Earthbounds:

  • Darklords: Good ATK and nice Levels but I don't feel them powerful enough.
  • Gravekeepers: Konami pls, just buff this guys for the love of Sophia…
  • Labrynth: I don't know the archetype well enough to know if it benefits from the morganites.
  • Reptilianne: Morgana's third effect can set up Recoil, Echidna, Vaskii, Scylla, Hydra and Viper.

Cards for further testing:

  • Darkest Diabolos, Lord of the Lair 
  • Cracking Dragon 
  • Pandemic Dragon 
  • The Big Saturn 
  • Gandora the Dragon of Destruction 
  • The End of Anubis 
  • Belial - Marquis of Darkness 
  • Invader of Darkness 
  • Jinzo 
  • Cosmo Queen the Queen of Prayers 
  • Great Long Nose 
  • Zombie World 

Maybe I overcooked this a little. Any techs and suggestions are welcome!

Ever wanted to summon 6 Synchro Monsters in 1 turn... In Edison?

To win a game with a board where every monster zone has a Synchro Monster?

Today I bring you  Ṭ̨͈̯͉͕̜̦̠͇̞̭̗̑̆ͦ̇ͩ̇̆͌ͭ͐ͮ̚͘͘̚ͅh̃ͣ͠ȩ̡̡͔͔̦̥͇̺ͣ̈̀͛͋̌ P̞̥͢ḭ̵̵̡̢̛̛̥͙̪͔̘̳̦̖̫̻̙̬͙̼͌̐͒̀͆̓̆̿̉ͯͯ́ͤ̔̀̆ͯͮ̕͞l̵̵̴̥̙̣̭͈̞͔̠̀ͩ̃ͭ͗͒ͤ̎ͥ̿͑̆̚͜͜͠͡ě̢̡̛͙̻̯̳̞̘͙̯̖ͯ̅̑͒̇̌̇̐̽ͪ͐̐̈́͑̉̌͜͟͟͢͞  :D :D

Q: What in Ra am I looking at?
A: The Pile :D

Q: No but really what does this thing even do?
A: It finds [Magical Merchant], all the while fending off the opponent's plays with Synchros, and when the time is right, it sets [Magical Merchant], mills 40 cards, and takes a turn so combo-licious that it'd make a 2026 Synchro-player Blush! :D

Q: How am I supposed to find a [Magical Merchant] in this mess??
A: First, you have a 5% chance of just winning the game turn 1 which is nice... But! If you don't draw Merchant immediately, you have many searchers! In fact, if you factor in all ways to Merchant, you have about an ~81% chance of opening a way to it!

Q: Okay, but what if I don't wanna play set and pass all game?
A: With all the combo extension tools that you have (Deepsea Diva, Blackwings, Zombies, Gallus, and the Flamvells), you have loaaads of way to start synchro-summoning right off the bat! 

Q: Why would I expose myself or my loved ones to this horror?
A: Scaring people is fun :D

Q: Okay but surely this can't be optimal, you have like [Time Wizard] in the deck!
A: There's nothing more fun than seeing your opponent wince as they realise they have to [Solemn Judgment] your [Time Wizard] as to not risk losing a game to a [Time Wizard] >:D

Q: Okay, sure, but there's no way this deck wins though, right?
A: >:)

Q: There's no way this wins, right??
A: >:)

Q: You're telling me that this PILE can compete??
A: Weeellll... It depends on your opponents. Really good players will understand how to smoke this deck after a game or two, once they realise what you're up to... But less experienced players, or players who aren't in a mindspace to deal with eldrich trickery... They will probably fear this thing.

Q: So I don't have to worry about this thing at tournaments at least, then.
A: >:)

Q: Oh you got to be kidding me why would you put yourself and others through this??
A: >:D >:D

deck_excerpt: Super cheap but enough to reach silver 1 on MD as a beginner player.
deck_description:
A simple concept that doesn't feel overpowered or unfair.

All roads lead to Bloom Diva, and this deck has enough gas in the tank to try more than once to get her on the board. good, because she will be your main survival tool (also her destruction protection makes Ostinato an infinetely better card, since she remains on the board).

I've included Mutiny In The Sky because using the GY for Fusion materials is an excellent way to rebound, plus so many Melodious cards go into the deck to special summon so having a way of putting some of the best ones back into that pool is great value. AND the spell card itself is very resilient as you can pretty easily put it back in your hand if it gets negated.

deck_description: A good blind second deck to play with during the dice rally. Really struggles with droll, but other than that, it's pretty solid. The main points within the deck are baiting and board breaking the opponent's board. The charmies are best to gain card advantage, and the usual board breakers like lightning storm, heavy storm, super poly, and droplet to break boards. Two AG fusion spells for better fodder and consistency, but can be swapped out for a different consistency card, like another urgent or droplet. Called by and Crossout are banned in this event, so no Droll in the deck. Verte is optional, but Saint Azamina is highly recommended. Pretty much it tbh, wishing for more AG support to better grind through boards.

deck_excerpt: 🍻It came to me in a dream :D :D🍻
deck_description:
1) Summon [Neiroy] (The Fiend) ➜ [Neiroy] (The Fairy) + Turn [Neiroy] (The Fiend) into Light ➜ Special Summon [Neiroy] (The Fairy)

2) Activate [Neiroy] (The Fiend) ➜ Tribute [Neiroy] (The Fairy) ➜ Special [Ikelos] + Add [Dream Mirror of Terror] through [Neiroy] (The Fiend)

3) On Summon, activate [Ikelos] ➜ Add [Dream Mirror of Chaos] ➜ Activate [Dream Mirror of Terror]

4) Link Summon [Fiendsmith Requiem], please do not use it yet.

5) Activate [Dream Mirror of Chaos], which can now banish from the GY ➜ Summon [Oneiros] (The Fiend)

🎉Tadaaaa, you can now play without worrying about your friend's nibiru🎉

Q: Why play this instead of a good Fiendsmith deck?
A: Dream mirror :D

Q: Okay, but what's with the weird side deck?
A: Those are the other Dream Mirror cards, ranked in order of most playable to least playable, in case you want to run more fun Dream Mirrors and less Solfas or Fiendscmidts :D

Q: Neat. Actually, why are there Solfachords here in the first place?
A: They make it easier to blend different archetypes together, since they're so generic! Think of them as a free extra "normal" (special) summon!

Q: What does a pure Dream Mirror deck look like though?
A: Eehhh try to keep them somewhat mixed with other decks, just trust me on that. I spent way too much time trying to make pure Dream Mirror work back in 2022 and even back then they were near unplayable.

Q: Hah, you can't stop me 
A: ... Here you dropped this, king👑
First you crash the monsters, then you mash the monsters, then you have yourself a lovely little side-dish! :D :D

deck_excerpt: THE MASTER OF FASTER HAS ENTERED GENESYS FORMAT!!!
deck_description:
Jack Atlas' Red Dragon Archfiend has received it's most powerful wave of support yet with the release of Blazing Dominion, and it's ready to burn a path of destruction through the GENESYS format! The new cards are minimally pointed in GENESYS with Power Vice Dragon only costing 5 points apiece and The Crimson King costing 10 points - meaning you can shove in the strategy a powerful sub engine!

My sub engine of choice is Elfnote because it has the ability to extend synchro plays, insulate combos with Crystal Wing's monster negate and offers additional interruption. Interestingly, both archetypes can branch into the other, since the Elfnote Power Patron is a fiend - making it searchable with Soul Resonator and Crimson Call! Elfnote can also make generic level 7s and 8s, so it can make Dark Dragon Synchros to support Red Dragon Archfiend combos. 

The uninterrupted endboards for this strategy are near impenetreble, featuring Hot Red Abyss's targeted omni-negate, Bystial Dis Pater's monster negate/destroy, Red Zone's targeted destruction, Crystal Wing's monster negate, Elfnote Rhapsodia's onmi-negate, Tinia's handloop, Lucina's bounce, and more! There is also near-infinite follow-up with both engines searching each other's combo pieces/starters every turn, and looping each other from the GY/Banishment. 

This combo juggernaut is surely worthy of a Crimson King. Are you ready to obliterate your opponents as the Master of Faster with your ABSOLUTE POWERFORCE? 

GLHF Duelists! 

Want to see the deck in action? Scroll to the very bottom for the Youtube video!

deck_excerpt: “Naturias with Horus’ Spam” – Control and Troll!
deck_description:
Just got back to the game after a long break and decided to build a deck I haven’t played in a while—Naturias, with Horus as support. So far, the deck runs really well and is a lot of fun to play.

 

It can struggle a bit against decks that rely heavily on banishing, but like any deck, it can be improved over time. I’m still learning some of the newer cards and hope to have an even stronger version ready soon. Always open to suggestions and love hearing tips from others!

Deck list made by someone that REALLY plays with the deck (contrary to what you generally see online), using the actual relevant cards for this Gaia. The best Gaia archetype is "Gaia the Dragon Champion" sub-archetype, a going-second deck that depends heavily on your Normal Summon and fusions (you should have some 4-6 in the main deck for that purpose, here's 4.5). As you may be aware, the deck barely leaves the casual tier, even though it's an OTK deck. 

The deck suffers from HT or interactions, so I added Terraforming, Shrine, Gateway to Chaos and Instant Fusion to bait and overcome some of them. Curse of Dragon Fusion is a target for Instant Fusion even outside Gaia decks, but that card can also be used to fusion the relinquished engine to 2 steal monsters or protect youself against HT; AND to work a body for tribute summoning.

Engines to work with: Primite (beryl takes your normal summon but can be used to fusion Sky Galloping; Lode generally is used at MP2), Branded and Dracotail (both are way too much dominant, though). Black Luster Soldier Gaia deck is thrash, dont bother mixing.

deck_description:
Do you hate the current Master Duel meta? Feel like wanting to slap someone through the screen after every match? Just want to make them eat their own stupid boss monster omni-negates?

Look no further! We have Chimera Hecahands Azamina, or as I like to call it, CHA-CHA. With Hecahands, we'll steal their boss monsters, then Azamina and Chimera will negate all of their effects.

You may be asking yourself: "How could these archetypes work together?" The answer: Nightmare Apprentice and Illusion monsters.

Nightmare Apprentice searches all of your Illusion monsters, and the only non-illusion monsters you have in this deck are card starters and handtraps. Best of all, all of these decks use Illusion monsters.

Hecahands will steal with Dandalos and Xeno all of your opponent's boss monsters. Azamina and Chimera the Illusion Beast will reduce their attack to 0 and negate all of their effects permanently. 

First, second, CHA-CHA doesn't care. We don't need to destroy their monsters, we'll just steal them and attack them directly with their own monsters. So relax, grab popcorn, and enjoy the show.

👑 QUEEN OF HARPIES HAS RETURNED 👑 Mai Valentine is back with her deadliest Harpie build yet! Fast combos, brutal swarming, elegant pressure, and explosive OTK potential — this deck turns every duel into a storm of feathers and chaos. 🦅⚡ In this video: 🔥 Powerful Harpie combos 🔥 Stylish EDOPro gameplay 🔥 Insane comeback moments 🔥 Competitive swarm strategy 🔥 Anime vibes with Mai Valentine herself Beauty. Elegance. Deadly. The Harpie Ladies never lost their throne. 💜

Had this idea for months but never had time to play in locals/tournaments to prove the theory.

The breakdown on this deck's theory:

The main deck is what I've been playing on Master Duel, but the actual OCG/TCG main deck theory is to play Chimera in main while side decking all take-control cards. The idea is "What is yours, is mine, and what is mine, is still mine."

The Master Duel Deck (Take Control) goes permanantly on going 2nd, resolving any Mulcharmies / Maxx "C" / Dimension Shifter before Main Phase. Taking advantage of Hecahands Dandalos monster bypass effect which allows "Hecahands" Fusion monsters and OPPONENT OWNED monsters to do direct attacks, if done right, is an easy OTK. The goal is not to take another turn, either you win or quit. The weaknesses are duplicates on hand, no Hecahands to fusion, no/low monster count type decks (e.g. Sky Strikers). As you are trying to OTK and not take another turn, Hecahands' counter traps serves no functions and should not be included. ALSO, you see no Ash because every card on your hand is extremely important and you want monster cards on their field as well. Trying to block let say Branded Fusion might actually cause you to lose out on a monster for an OTK. So it is better strategically and tactically better to refrain from adding Single-Negate type handtraps. IT"S JUST NOT WORTH IT.

OCG/TCG Deck theory instead is a dynamic round-based tactic, relying on switching between a general Midrange deck (chimera hybrid version) to an OTK deck (Full Hecahands + Take Control cards), becoming a strong 2nd turn deck and catch the opponent off-guard if they are given to choose and they choose to go first. This version has a totally different approach to playing the deck, so the above deck theory for Master Duel do no apply here. So you can include Single-Negate type handtraps and Hecahands' counter traps (not shown above), side decking out when no longer needed.


All Side Decks cards shown are a showcase of potential suitable cards that you can feel free to include.

deck_excerpt: Core Gameplan  The deck revolves around:  generating extra Tribute material, Tribute Summoning powerful bosses, disrupting the opponent whil
deck_description:
Core Gameplan

The deck revolves around:

  • generating extra Tribute material,
  • Tribute Summoning powerful bosses,
  • disrupting the opponent while gaining card advantage,
  • and locking the opponent out of mechanics they rely on.

Unlike combo decks that try to build huge boards in one turn, Monarchs play a more methodical game.

How the Deck Functions

1. Set Up Tribute Material

Small monsters like:

  • Edea the Heavenly Squire
  • Eidos the Underworld Squire

help you get extra summons and Tribute fodder quickly.

A common opening:

  • Normal Summon Edea
  • Special Summon Eidos from deck
  • Gain an extra Tribute Summon
  • Tribute into a Monarch immediately

This lets the deck start applying pressure on turn 1 with relatively few cards.

My goal with this deck is to attempt to repeatedly summon Charmers of the Grand Circle throughout the game as I expect at least one person to have an answer to the first summon. I'm also messing around with beatdown strategies that reward the player for bringing out as many attributes as possible. It's not perfect, and the best ideas were shamelessly taken from random YouTube comments. Either way, this may be the foundation for something.

1. Summon two monsters

2. Link Summon Crowley the First Propheseer

3. Reveal Spellbook Library of the Crescent, Spellbook of Secrets, and Spellbook of the Grand Circle

4. If you're unlucky and get Spellbook Library, reveal more cards. I'd pick Spellbook of Secrets, Spellbook of the Grand Circle, and in a worst case scenario, Spellbook of Power

5. Search out the Charmers you need with Spellbook of the Grand Circle, but make sure Dharc is one of them

6. Summon Charmers of the Grand Circle, mostly focusing on the revival effect, and be sure Dharc is one of the revived

7. Send Dharc and Crowley to the GY, special summoning Familiar Possessed Dharc

8. Familiar Possessed Dharc's effect adds Matchgiru to hand

9. Because Dharc has the right ATK value, Matchgiru can be special summoned

10. Matchgiru searches for... something. This is where my plan starts to fall apart, as I'm not sure where to actually go from here

11. Maybe go for Fairy Tail Ball for a negate or Tails of the Fairy Tails for ATK boost or a fusion?

12. And there is more you can do, as plenty of the things you can link climb into have revival effects

13. I guess try to OTK, and if your field is broken, hope to fusion summon again, somehow

14. That's all I've got for now

deck_description:
It turns out that one of the best ways to play Snake-Eyes right now is with Yugi/Atem's Shining Sarcophagus archetype, rendering these fan-favourite nostalgia bait cards actually playable in the cutthroat Masterduel metagame!

Diabellstar and her level one pyro snakes are back thanks to the recently released Dark Magician cards! Diabellstar can easily turn into Dragoon thanks to Dark Magician of Destruction, allowing the pilot to fully combo under it's protective omni-negate!

In fact, every level 6 or higher dark spellcaster can become Dark Magician of Destruction, which can search the new fusion spell Gaze of Timaeus. Gaze of Timaeus shuffles back fusion materials (including Dark Magician) from the field/GY to fusion summon Dragoon! Dark Magician of Destruction is also a level 8, so when it's paired up with the Shining Sarcophagus Engine's other level 8 with Gandora-G, they can XYZ summon The Zombie Vampire, which gets your Snake-Eyes and Fiendsmith combo lines started! 

Under the protective negation umbrella that Dragoon offers, you can easily shrug off pesky turn 0 handtraps like Izuna and Hollie Sue, or turn ending cards like Nibiru! The consistency boost with the free Zombie Vampire mill + 2 bodies is also an incredible boon for Snake-Eyes as well! Before long, your opponent will be staring down an entire field of Snake-Eye boss monsters, including the invincible, untargetable and indestructible Dragoon! 

Want to see the deck in action? Scroll to the very bottom for the Youtube video!

deck_name: Chimera, Ritual of Light and Darkness, Fiendsmith (50 Card Board Breaker)
deck_excerpt: Teocrafing deck with the new Ritual of Light and Darkness engine
deck_description:
The main concept of this deck is that you can chain multiple archetypes together through several engine bridges.

It starts with the well-known synergy between Chimera and Fiendsmith. Using the Fusion Summon effect of “Berfomet the Mythical King of Phantom Beasts,” you can dump “Evil HERO Sinister Necrom” into the GY. Necrom can then banish itself to Special Summon “Evil HERO Adusted Gold” from the Deck, giving you access to a LIGHT Fiend for the Fiendsmith line.
From there, you can perform the standard Fiendsmith combo to summon “Fiendsmith’s Sequence”, which allows you to Fusion Summon “Aerial Eater” using Fiendsmith monsters in your GY as material. “Aerial Eater” then dumps “Skull Archfiend of Chaos” to start your “Ritual of Light and Darkness” combo.
You Ritual Summon “Magician of Dark Chaos – Black Chaos” to recover one of your Spells from the GY, usually “Chimera Fusion”. You can then banish the Ritual Monster to bridge into the “Dark Magician of Destruction” engine.
After that, you can link away “Fiendsmith’s Sequence” to make “W:P Fancy Ball.” During your opponent’s turn, after “Red-Eyes Dark Dragoon” has already used its effect, you can link Dragoon and Fancy Ball into “Charmer Quartet in Bloom” reviving both Dragoon and Fancy Ball to put two negates back online.

You can also ignore the Fiendsmith engine entirely if you open Chimera. In that case, you can dump “Skull Archfiend” off Berfomet directly to avoid giving your opponent draws through the Mulcharmies.

One of the coolest aspects of the deck is that “Nightmare Hands” and “Foolish Burial” become one-card combos if you dump “Fleeting Phantom Mask Master” and then banish the Spell used to send it.

That combo line works like this:

Dump Phantom
Summon Phantom by banishing a Spell
Use Phantom’s effect to dump Necrom
Go into the Fiendsmith line
Instead of summoning a Fiendsmith monster off Sequence, summon Berfomet instead
Dump Skull Archfiend
Continue into the Ritual of Light and Darkness and Dark Magician of Destruction engines

TL;DR of the engine bridges:

Chimera → Ritual of Light and Darkness
Chimera → Fiendsmith
Fiendsmith → Ritual of Light and Darkness
Ritual of Light and Darkness → Dark Magician of Destruction

Also, note that the side deck is mostly a list of optional Extra Deck alternatives and two floodgates you can use going first. Some choices are better than others. You do not really need “Constellar Ptolemy M7” or “Fiendsmith’s Agnumday” but cards like “Cross-Sheep” or “Magnum the Reliever” would have been very good if the Extra Deck space were larger.

deck_excerpt: What if you could trigger 2 Tzolkin effects on your opponents turn
deck_description:
I wanted to test the ceiling of the assault mode engine and I ended up with an absolute monstrosity

"One card" combo:

- Start with psi reflector/field spell, search the other piece, then search for assault beast (pay 2000 LP for the field spell now to tribute from ED for assault modes later down the line)

- Pitch assault beast and revive w/ psi reglecter to make nightwing cleric

- Set assault mode activate from deck, immediately activate to summon Big Stardust

- Discard a card to summon assault lich, re-summon beast and make Odd-Eyes Solblaze dragon (you'll add assault counter from lich)

- Make tzolkin, set assault counter to summon victim sanctuary

- GY eff of solblaze to resummon and make the 2nd tzolkin, set the assault mode activate to summon AFD

- AFD pops field spell for world of spirits, activate it to grab spore and summon it with AFD (do not activate its eff)

- banish victim sanctuary from GY (condition satisfied from tributing stardust from ED) to summon accel stardust.

On the opponents turn, you can use accel synchro to summon stardust dragon and make another nightwing cleric using AFD and spore, which will set Assault Mode Activate from GY and proc both of your tzolkins to summon more negates (you'll want to negate something with big stardust before commiting to the accel synchro so you actually have enough board space) and you can also trigger assault mode activate again to make a 2nd big stardust.

LMK if you have any suggestions about the combo line! the endboard is clogged with a bunch of synchros so if you pull off the above on ur opponents turn you only have enough board space to resummon one big stardust provided none of ur other monsters get cleared; this feels bad but if none of ur guys were cleared by the end of ur opponents turn then ur probably winning anyways so shrug.

deck_excerpt: 🍺Did you know that Ikelos is a Dark Fairy?🍺
deck_description:
🍺I might'be gone a bit rogue with this one🍺

Q: What? What even is this?
A: It's not that good, but it's fun I promise!

1) [Neiroy] (Fiend) ➔ [Neiroy] (Fairy) + [Dream Mirror of Terror]

2) Tribute [Neiroy] (Fairy) ➔ [Ikelos] (Light) ➔ [Dream Mirror of Chaos]

3) Activate [Dream Mirror of Terror] + Activate [Ikelos] (Light) ➔ [Ikelos] (Dark, Fairy)

4) Do something with [Neiroy] (Fiend) if you can. For example, synchro him off with [Allvain] for an extra Dark Fairy you could use, if you didn't use [Allvain] for [Ancient Fairy Dragon] or something else

?) Fusion summon by banishing the materials from your GY using [Dream Mirror of Chaos] while [Dream Mirror of Terror] is on the field

⬤ If you didn't draw [Brilliant Fusion] but you drew [Ready Fusion], you can synchro with [Neiroy] and [Allvain] into [Accel Synchron], activate its effect and send [Jet Synchron] to increase its level to 6, then syncrho summon using [Accel Synchron] and [Ikelos] into [Ancient Fairy Dragon] and then use that to Special Summon your [Darklord Djehuti] 

⬤ If you use [Brilliant Fusion] for an extra normal summon, you can either use [Seraphinite] for an XYZ summon, or you can Synchro it into [Ancient Fairy Dragon] with [Allvain] for even more special summons, or you can [Black Rose Dragon] if you need to boom💥

deck_excerpt: Did you know Purrely can search the Heralds?
deck_description:
Did you know any hand that opens Kambi + any other way to a lv1 fairy (usually a Purrely) can guaranteed search both Heralds? Once you have a lv1 fairy on field with Kambi in hand, the combo goes as follows:

1. Kambi eff to summon from hand

2. Kambi eff tribute itself to summon Eva from deck and lock yourself into fairies

3. Overlay Eva with your other lv1 fairy to summon Shamoji Soldier

4. Activate Shamoji Soldier detaching Eva (either effect works but the LP gain is safer to play around your opponent drawing into a kaiju)

5. Trigger Eva in GY banishing Kambi from GY and Shamoji Soldier from field to search Orange & Green Light (can banish other light fairies from GY instead of Shamoji from field if you have them, but I usually prefer banishing Shamoji from field anyway so my lv1 Purrely doesn't get stuck underneath it)

Shamoji Soldier is here bc it's the only fairy rank 1 that exists (conveniently also light attribute) and is therefore necessary for the combo line. Its effect is irrelevant beyond the fact that it is always activateable and detaches a material.
My attempt at a Modern Eldlich Deck

deck_excerpt: The Winged Dragon of Ra's OTK
deck_description:
The Winged Dragon of Ra and Horus, with their respective engines, form a combo deck whose main goal is to summon Ra with Ancient Chant and power him up with Blaze Cannon, resulting in an easy OTK.

The True Sun God is the main card of Ra's engine and the beating heart of the Deck, able to search the Winged Dragon of Ra or any other card that mentions it in its text with its activation: Guardian Slime, Ra's Disciple, Immortal Pheonix, Sphere Mode, Ancient Chant, Blaze Cannon, Millennium Revelation, and Sun God Unification are all searchable by TTSG. In the case of Guardian Slime, the opposite is also true: by sending this card from the field or hand to the graveyard, TTSG can be searched, and with it, other cards can be searched too. TTSG is also an important Floodgate because it prevents all monsters in play from attacking the turn they are Special Summoned, except the Winged Dragon of Ra itself. Last but not least, TTSG's effect summons Immortal Phoenix, sending Ra from the field to the graveyard.

The Horus Engine relies on sending cards from the hand to GY with King's Sarcophagus to send Horus monsters from the deck to the graveyard. These monsters are then Special Summoned from the GY if King's Sarcophagus is on the field. These four Special Summoned monsters are all level 8, potential sacrifices for Ra's summoning, materials for rank 8 XYZ monsters, or ammunitions for Blaze Cannon's ATK boost. Walls of the Imperial Tomb allows you to search for Imsety, which you can discard from your hand along with another card (good with another Horus monster or even better with Guardian Slime) to search for the Sarcophagus and start the Horus Engine.

Cards like Trade-In and Tokusano Shinkiogin allow you to draw 2 cards by sending Horus monsters and Guardian Slime or Immortal Phoenix from your hand to the graveyard respectively, ensuring greater fluidity and speed for the deck.

Optimal OTK combo: Tribute Summon the Winged Dragon of Ra with Ancient Chant using 3 Ra's Disciples as tributes, activate its power-up effect with your life points, then use King's Sarcophagus to summon the Horus monsters from the graveyard and finally activate Blaze Cannon on Ra's attack: the Horus monsters are tributed and increase Ra's attack to stellar levels, defeating the opponent with a single attack (Ra, thanks to Blaze Cannon, is also immune to the effects of the opponent's cards).

Sphere Mode and Soul Crossing (or Soul Exchange) should be used to Tribute the opponent's monsters if there are any dangerous elements and/or monsters capable of interrupting the various Engines' combos, while also clearing the board for a direct attack from the Winged Dragon of Ra.

Strengths: Fast deck, high pressure, high-level monsters, easy Xyz Summon of Rank 8 monsters, Boss Monster with an enormous attack power.

Weaknesses: Very susceptible to interruptions, approximately a 3% chance of Bricking due to the large number of high-level monsters (tested with Main Deck cards but not Side Deck cards).

Finally this is a deck that prefers to go second to better fulfill its purpose of OTK the opponent, but even going first isn't bad because you can starts your combos with less pressure of interruptions on the opponent's siede of the field.

If you want to try it, I hope you enjoy it: it's not a meta deck, but rather a fun deck, and it's also themed.

deck_excerpt: As close to full power combos as we can get
deck_description:
RDA takes a heavy hit in Genesys by not being able to play the Bystial engine off of Crimson Blade. After searching around I think the best alternative we can search is Levianeer. It enables us to rip a card out of the opponents hand going first and still get an 8 star for synchro material and it banishes something for Bystial Dis Pater to summon back. Also going second we can use it's double pop effect if we started with the Centur-ion engine because we will have a light in grave. 

The Shining Star Dragon is in there to make King Calamity during the opponents turn. In order to set this up all we need is Red Rising Dragon in grave and a 2 star Resonator on the field, this setup can be done easily and the biggest downside is that Shining Star Dragon is only during main phase and King Calamity can miss timing. You have to be careful of when you activate Shining Star Dragon.

One budget choice I made was playing 3 Stand-Up Centur-ion, if you have the Emblema Oaths or don't mind purchasing them you can easily go down to one copy of the field spell and play some Emblema Oaths and that would save you 10 points that you can use on better hand traps whatever other staples you like. I am using the Heavenly Zephyr because I think it is a decent 0 point option and I am using the Kurikara as a test for a board breaker. The non engine can be swapped out easily for whatever low cost staples you like. 

Feel free to let me know your thoughts, this is an expirimental list and you can easily cut things like the shining star and king calamity for some better non engine but I was trying to think outside the bun with this one.

deck_excerpt: Warrior Beatdown deck for Yu-Gi-Oh GX The Beginning of Destiny

deck_description:
Just a fun deck based on Archfiends and Royal Decree.

Some notes about the monsters choices::

- Terrorking Archfiend: The boss monster of the deck and the best Archfiend monster card. 

- Infernalqueen is a nerfed version of Terrorking and a good beater that can be searched by Sangan.

- Archfiend Soldier is the monster of choice to put preassure early in the game while Pandemonium isn't in the field,

- Desrock Archfiend is a decent hand trap that make Terrorking a bit more hard to deal with.

- Marauding Captain helps to summonTerrorking more easily.

- Spy is just Spy: Top 5 cmonster card in GOAT. Amazing defensive wall and also amazing in offensive when it's paired with a beater.

- Breaker, Tribe, D.D Warrior Lady, Exiled are just staples that help dealing with multiples threats.

- Sangan: Our recruiter.

Side needs more work but there's a lot of good cards in there.

Feel free to make suggestions of cards for the Main/Side. Just here to learn :).

Cheers.

deck_description:
Tournament: Venezuela National Championship (WCQ Caracas 2026)

Player: Gabriel Ramos

Result: Top 16 (13th Place out of Swiss)

Final Record: 8 wins - 3 losses

 

---

 

### Tournament Run & Deck Overview

This is the official Red Dragon Archfiend (RDA) deck list used to secure a Top 16 finish at the 2026 Venezuelan National Championship in Caracas. 

 

The strategy focuses on high-impact Synchro plays, maximizing the consistency of the Resonator engine to establish powerful boards capable of breaking established fields or locking down the opponent. The list was carefully teched for the current TCG metagame, adapting both the Main Deck distribution and Side Deck utility choices to handle the most prominent competitive matchups during the 11 grueling rounds of the tournament.

deck_excerpt: Fire Format burns through a Kewl Tunes dominated Masterduel ladder!
deck_description:
THE FIRE FORMAT FLAMES AREN'T EXTINGUISHED! It still burns strong in Masterduel ladder, despite the tier 1 presence of Kewl Tunes! It doesn't matter if they look at your hand, destroy your cards or stack your deck - your gas will burn through every interaction they have! 

It's flames are still alive and well like a Phoenix (or Garunix) rising from the ashes! It turns out that despite the many banlist hits effectively extinguishing Snake-Eyes, Fire King can be used as a crutch to put the heat back into this fire format strategy! Dust off the good ol' Snake-Eyes Ash, Promethean Princess, Hiita the Fire Charmer and Bonfire, FIRE FORMAT ISN'T EXTINGUISHED YET!

Fire King provides so much extension, recursion, bodies and utility to the Snake-Eyes strategy. Almost EVERYTHING floats you into either more giant beatsticks on field or more resources in-hand. Piloted properly, Snake-Eye Fire King definitely can compete in ladder where K9, Mitsurugi, Dracotail and Ryzeal are dominant! It can also deal with Maxx "C," counter stun decks, and dominate every strategy with it's massive bodies, recursion and resources!

Want to see the deck in action? Scroll to the very bottom for the Youtube video!

If you have any questions about this deck, feel free to ask me in the comments below!

Tournament: Chile National Championship 2026 – May 16th 2026

Placement: Top 4

Well, we're back to Maliss despite hit after hit after hit, its still going and providing a strong argument as a top 5 deck.  The engine is still as brutal as ever, but doesn't nearly have the grind game anymore, espectially without Binder.  Combos are still pretty similar to what they used to be, but now can work in Heatsoul as an extra couple draws to find more non-engine, then be sacrificed to Allied's Omni.  the non-engine lineup has also seen a boost with the addition of Dominus Spark, though the deck is much cheaper without it at its currently $75-80 price tag.  Only having 6 1 card combos hurts, but Backup or Chessy+ Hare or a Bystial gets you playing Ash/Droll+Backup works as well with Almiraj, even Chessy and Backup do it, so plenty of 2-2.5 card combos keep the deck consistent.  Shifter along with Retaliating "C", Anti-Spell and D Fissure in the side can just win by themselves into some matchups.  Mistaken Arrest is essentially extra copies of Droll that work more proactively going first.  Duster kinda just got in due to needing to get a 15th card with some power.  Talents and Called By are always nice to side in to punish hand traps, though with the Dominus cards and Harmonia in the format i don't feel they are quite consistent enought to main.  Belle i'm not entirely sure about, it could easily be something else like Ogre or maybe Droplets, but its definitely playable enough this format.  Maliss mainly benefits from no longer having a giant target on its back with Meowls and Shifter out of side boards, and Kewl Tune and Branded especially struggling to play under D Fissure and playing into Retaliating "C", giving solid Matchups into the top 2 decks while still being able to nearly auto-win going first.

Attempt at cooking a Lunalight Decklist for Genesys Format

The Unchained engine can pivot into the Fiendsmith engine by Xyz Summoning Evilswarm Exciton Knight.

Unchained Ogre Shara + Unchained Ogre Shma = Exciton Knight

Unchained Ogre Shma and D/D/D Wave High King Caesar are Water Attribute monsters, so you can Tribute them to activate Spiritual Water Art - Aoi. Triple Tactics Thrust can search for Spiritual Water Art - Aoi. Unchained Enma Lord Yama can special summon Unchained Ogre Shma from your graveyard during either player's End Phase if it was destroyed and sent there during that turn.

deck_excerpt: Who else misses Kash. (this wouldn't have happened if i was playing birrrrrrrds)
deck_description: Did the Kash bans make you sad? Are you thinking about playing Ojama's? Well here is another opption! This deck can zone block (by summoning tokens), can on sideded board wipe turn after turn, and consistantly lets you watch as your opponets life leaves from their eyes as they cant play the game of yugioh while they sit across from you.

Somewhat slow start. I am trying to work on this deck before actually buying it and more consistent into summoning all 3 gods. I know the best way to win is with the 2 super dreadnoughts in the extra deck but idc that much, it's a good work around for monsters/extra deck that say monsters cannot be destroyed by battle or something similar.

Besides mulcharmy negates this deck really focuses on all gas and no pedal.

Everything is bait for the crystal beasts and if you do get stuff off, then you get potential interaction or nice extenders. 

Please ask about any choices, and the side deck is more different choices and the actual side deck choices are ROTA and radiant typhoon stuff to get more spells in the GY + draw more. You can also side out a rainbow bridge of the heart/ one delta/ crossout/ called by if need be/ seldom (very seldom) one bond. Delta is easily the first cut as you can search it other ways.

Foolish goods is full combo, pegasus is almost full combo (still really good), and stiker can search/ draw into this stuff as well to get more draws from radiant stuff.

Please give advice, loving crystal beasts rn

Possible changes

  • Cut Virus cards from side. Too high of an investment
  • Cut Arias to 1

Things I liked

  • Did not miss Trap Trick 
  • Songs overperfomed but I am still not sold on it being in the main

Notes

  • Dark Sacrifice is a Normal Trap out to Lightning Storm/Duster, which everyone is siding, and it dumps furniture. If you are scared of Ash, you can swap for something like Starlight Road. But Dark Sacrifice also works on effects that pop the opponent's cards, like the DoomZ effects
  • Would like to try Fydraulis in the main once I get them

deck_name: [ Top 32 WCQ VENEZUELA NATIONAL CHAMPIONSHIP ] Orcust mitsurugi
deck_description:
Top 32 in the Venezuelan national 2026. 

 

Simplify, optimize, and boost. That was the strategy behind creating the deck. Orcust is primarily a deck with a long and often problematic grind. 

With cards like Knightmare and Wand, the deck suffers from inconsistency in mixed recipes, and having Crown in hand, the field and the counter trap are a recipe for not playing. That's why I decided to remove Wand and Knightmare from the deck, and maximize the normal summons that Orcust offers, with Cymbal being the worst of them, but still enough to reach an Omni negator. And carrying two Cymbals is because many times I ended up not having enough names to grind with. 

With Mitsurugi, something curious happens: I decided to keep the trap outside the main deck because you already had a negating trap. If I draw both of them in my hand, it's unplayable. I never lacked Mitsurugi names in my deck; in fact, I often had too many. Mitsurugi has its bridge to the orcust with the Vampire Zombie and dingirsu, which was indisputably the best monster in the deck. Whenever I was able to solve dingirsu, I ended up winning the duel (or the Match). The decisions of the side are very specific. If I saw that it was a deck that prioritized the graveyard as a mechanic, there are DDG and Skull Meister. By deciding not to bring Lancea for a Maliss or a Mirror Match, or even for an Evenly Match, I found myself in a very difficult position in some duels, specifically two that I lost (Maliss in round 6 and Exosister in round 13).

The deck itself performs very well, and with Dominus Impulse it improves significantly. It's a deck that could do well in this format, and it remains to be seen how it performs.

📜 Mô Tả Tổng Quan

  • Định danh: Đội hình 100% Hiệp Sĩ Rực Lửa (Hệ Lửa / Tộc Chiến Binh).

  • Lối chơi chính: Combo (Múa chuỗi chiêu thức) kết hợp Control (Kiểm soát bàn đấu).

  • Cơ chế cốt lõi: Sử dụng các Phép Trang Bị (Equip Spell - Các thanh kiếm) để kích hoạt hiệu ứng, sau đó biến chính các hiệp sĩ dưới Mộ thành giáp trụ đắp lên người Boss để tăng tiến sức mạnh.

  • Điểm mạnh: Hình ảnh cực ngầu, Boss có khả năng cấm đoán Phép/Bẫy của đối thủ và sở hữu sức sát thương kết liễu khổng lồ chỉ trong một lượt.

  • Điểm yếu: Đội hình "thuần" nên phụ thuộc khá nhiều vào nhân phẩm ở 5 lá bài đầu tiên để có được chuỗi khởi đầu trơn tru.

⚔️ Cách Chơi (Chiến Thuật 3 Bước)

Lối chơi của bộ này vận hành như một dây chuyền rèn vũ khí, đi từ những nguyên liệu thô sơ (quái nhỏ) lên đến một pháo đài bọc thép bất khả chiến bại.

Bước 1: Dò đường & Khởi động

  • Mục tiêu: Bằng mọi giá phải đưa được bộ đôi Ogier và Renaud ra sân.

  • Cách làm: Dùng Ogier gửi bài xuống Mộ, sau đó Đặc triệu hồi Renaud từ tay xuống để nhặt lại bài. Nếu không có sẵn trên tay, hãy dùng kiếm Durendal (tự trang bị rồi tự phá hủy) để tìm 1 trong 2 lá này từ bộ bài.

Bước 2: Thăng cấp liên tục (Leo tháp Synchro)

  • Mục tiêu: Đưa quái thú từ cấp thấp lên cấp cao.

  • Cách làm: Dùng Ogier + Renaud Synchro ra Angelica -> Dùng hiệu ứng của Angelica gọi Captain Roland ra sân và ném Oliver/Turpin xuống Mộ -> Synchro tiếp lên Emperor Charles (Cấp 9).

Bước 3: Khoác giáp & Áp đảo (Giai đoạn End-game)

  • Mục tiêu: Dựng pháo đài và kết liễu địch.

  • Cách làm:

    1. Biến Emperor Charles thành Quái thú Link-1 Emperor Charles the Great (Boss tối thượng).

    2. Dùng hiệu ứng của Boss hút lại chính Emperor Charles từ Mộ lên làm áo giáp.

    3. Gắn thêm các hiệp sĩ từ dưới Mộ (như Ogier, Oliver) vào Boss để lấy các hiệu ứng bảo vệ (không thể bị phá hủy, không thể bị nhắm mục tiêu).

    4. Phòng ngự: Khi đối thủ xài Phép/Bẫy, Boss sẽ hủy kích hoạt chúng.

    5. Tấn công: Gắn thanh kiếm đôi Hauteclere vào Boss để chém 2 nhát (tổng 6000 sát thương), kết hợp cùng các hiệp sĩ nhỏ trên sân để quét sạch Điểm Gốc của đối thủ.

deck_excerpt: A powerful combo deck with lots of inspiration from https://ygoprodeck.com/deck/radiant-typhoon-tri-brigade-mitsurugi-yummy-702907#/
deck_description: This deck bridges from most of the archetypes into eachother, giving powerful end boards with an easy otk on the following turn. I decided to put Radiant Typhoon Krosea in the main deck as it adds more consistency with the recent limiting of Cupsy☆Yummy and Radiant Typhoon Chant while also giving a better position going second. One thing to note when learning and playing this deck is that when performing the mitsurugi combo when you are at the point where Mitsurugi no Mikoto, Saji  and Mitsurugi no Mikoto, Aramasa are on the board alone, you must first summon Sky Striker Ace - Camellia and Sky Striker Ace - Kagari before continuing the mitsurugi combo and avoid summoning a rank 4 as you need no monsters in your main monster zone for Sky Striker Mecha - Hornet Drones to be able to activate and prevent you from being able to bridge into the yummy combo.

deck_name: R.B. S.S. - Sky Striking Rebel Bots
deck_description:
Any 2 machines = full combo

Any 2 bodies = full combo? (sometimes - Sky Striker Ace - Camellia )

The Rebel Bots are getting some backup from the Sky Strikers, as they help us get 2 machines on the field and give us a few options when all else fails.

Pillar of the Future - Cyanos is a great normal summon full combo starter that can also be used as plan B special summon, when you have already used your normal summon.

Girsu, the Orcust Mekk-Knight is another excellent normal summon full combo, that you will feel great if it's on-summon effect gets Ashed (not so much when it is impermed). Linking it or the token off for a Galatea-I, the Orcust Automaton allows us to get the World Legacy - "World Crown" into hand and also gives an additional machine body while Galatea-I, the Orcust Automaton is in the GY.

Sky Striker Ace - Raye can also be a one card full combo if all else fails (you will not be able to use R.B. Stage Landing if you go this route, but it sometimes is not even needed). Using her effect to go into Sky Striker Ace = Zero and not using the on-summon effect, but the tribute effect gets you 2 bodies that can go into an OTK (R.B. The Brute Blues + R.B. Ga10 Pile Bunker) or a R.B. Shepherd's Crook + whatever you need if going first.
Usually you do NOT want to use the on-summon effect of Zero, since she cannot be linked off.

Since the deck is VERY weak to droll, we have the Crossout Designator  and standard Called by the Grave

If you have an extra Sky striker body (either of the main deck girls or the token), try to get a Prototype Sky Striker Ace - Amatsu on the field for an extra negate.

deck_excerpt: Moving monsters around with new and old cards. Turbo-illegal in Genesys.
deck_description: This decklist I've crafted attempts to revive the Vaylantz archetype with Elfnote/Medius support. The optimal route is starting with Medius (Hence 3 Medius, 3 Theorealize, 3 Small World, side 3 Vandalism). Synchro into Justiciar for the chain block search to Lucina. Search Regina into Fortuna to Aristea to set up a movement option. Link into Lyna with the lights, which triggers Justiciar's SS into Lee to add Guardragon. Succession lets you reborn a tuner, so pick what you want from the graveyard according to your situation. If you need a Vaylantz starter, summon Crocodragon for the extra card draw (consistently Power Patron + Lee + Regina for 2 draws). After finishing all your special summons for Elfnote and World Legacy, start combo with Vaylantz. You want to wait for this since they all lock your special summons. From there remember you have a limited number of searches, so pick each card carefully to continue setting up your endboard. Speaking of which, you won't always have the same endboard since it's dependent on how many Vaylantz cards you draw. That's about it, sideboard is pretty self-explainatory -  sub in what you prefer. This deck runs better in Master Duel since you have access to Electrumite there, though the TCG version is functional without it. Have fun confusing yourself and your opponents!

Turn 2 Kozmo strategy with generic support for the archetype. You can add more Kozmo monsters if the deck feels too thin but this is a 40 card list for those which are picky. Second round if you suspect you'll go first, side in the traps of your choice. Waking the Dragon is particularly nice since you can grab any Kozmo and its on-summon effect off it. Swords and Mirror Force can be good against other turn 2 decks like Tenpai or Mikanko. Dark Destroyer is only really used for trap decks like Traptrix or Labrynth. No extra deck since many of the Ultimate Slayer targets are pointed high (though with some edits, that is a reasonable strategy as well).

deck_excerpt: Witchcrafters and Invoked take on Genesys
deck_description: Utilizing a control strategy with Witchcrafters and taking advantage of their multitude of attributes for Invoked.
Synchron time. Always chain block junk speeder with assault sonic.

deck_excerpt: New Witchcrafter Support! Lets GO! 17 Non-engine and 12 one card starters!
deck_description:
I've made some pretty big changes and I think this list is pretty close. 17 non-engine is exactly where I want it and the main deck is super tight.

The linear combo is quite simple (Check Youtube Link) but the interaction is layered and the follow up is great. We have 12 one card combos that center around the field spell. The Genni Maid of Endymion Line is slightly different than the rest but all lead to a very similar end board.

The side board is where this deck really shines: Access to the Magistus Zoroa Synchro gives us a D-Barrier on legs, and because this deck doesn't actually need its normal summon we can play lava golem as well. Surprisingly Macro type effects are also not the end of the world for this deck. Being able to fuse with banished spellcasters means that Retaliating "C" can be played with careful gameplay.

The Extra deck is tight, between Super Poly and the Magistus cards. Depending on the format the targets for those 2 engines could be cut or changed.

Please try this deck out! I truly believe this is the most playable version of Witchcrafters yet and I can't wait to bring this to events.

deck_name: The highway of aether and shadow

deck_description: Not sure this is the first time putting three archtypes into my deck but I digress.

deck_excerpt: R.B. - what does it stand for after all?
deck_description:
What does R.B. stand for? Really Bad? Rusty Bots? Rogue Bait?...

...Or, for those who can handle the pressure, Really Busted?

As I climbed through the ranked ladder this season, I'm inclined to say the latter is more accurate. The joke stopped being funny somewhere between Diamond and Master - this deck is actually serious.

R.B. Stage Landing is one of the most custom cards ever - non-once per turn starter and extender with a great bonus effect to protect your monsters from destruction. It is also easily accessible, since any 2 Machines can go into R.B. VALCan Booster (which searches Stage Landing), so you can play a bunch of different engines to make it happen. R.B. The Brute Blues also searches another copy of Stage Landing, making the deck come together.

As for what engines to play, Sky Striker is incredible. Pillar of the Future - Cyanos is basically Tour Guide for this deck, being a great normal summon (and, in a pinch, also an extender) that summons Sky Striker Ace - Roze from the deck or GY, and with the Sky Striker links you have 2 Machines (they are also 1500 ATK so they don't conflict with Stage Landing's restriction!). In addition to that, Sky Striker Mecha - Hornet Drones is also full combo if you burn through a couple Sky Striker link monsters.

Mecha Phantom Beasts is also a great choice, since it's cheap and has no bricks - you can normal summon Mecha Phantom Beast O-Lion, link into Clockwork Knight, summon a token and there you have your 2 Machines. Mecha Phantom Beast Tetherwolf is similar, summoning a token on normal summon. And what if you draw multiples? No problem, O-Lion in the GY gives you an additional normal summon for a Mecha Phantom Beast, giving you an additional extender.

Orcust and Speedroid packages are also options, but I prefer the Mecha Phantom Beast engine for the lack of bricks and no URs (other than Clockwork Knight).

All in all, I believe this is a solid deck, and not even that expensive if you already have the Sky Striker cards. Give it a try! Hope you enjoy the deck and the video, and let me know your thoughts on it!

I tried to make it as competitive as possible. I have calculated that you have a 91.93% chance of having a starter card in your first turn hand and the chance of having a starter card and extender 51.41%.

deck_name: DID I CATCH YOU MOVING FROM GY

deck_description:
Deck idea for a possible future Duel Links character Ghost (5D'S). I hope the KONAMI think kinda similar way as me :)

 

In the anime Ghost (or Diablo) is Primo's duelbot hive-mind and it's use the "Ally of Justice" archetype. I also gived him most of the "Genex Ally" sub-archetype monsters (except those what can use different attribute monsters such WIND, FIRE or WATER) and I know I gived those cards to Greiger + D. S. Greiger but honestly those cards is more fit for him (the "Ally" on the name; Machine type; DARK attribute - these same details as the "Ally of Justice" cards has). And finally cards that destroy monsters on your own field side (for Meklord Emperor Wisel). 

(Ace animated monster: Meklord Emperor Wisel)

deck_excerpt: Dungeons and Dragons deck that makes your opponent hate the game
deck_description:
Mimighoul is fun in the way you get to play the game and your opponent doesn't. Play your field spell, summon a few monsters in face-down defense mode to your opponents field, and watch them rage in being unable to normal summon or attack with any special summoned monsters. You can have even more fun by using Mimighoul Master to activate the following effects on your opponents turn by flipping up the following cards:

- Mimighoul Dragon - Destroys all your opponent's face up monsters.

- Mimighoul Cerberus - Banishes the top three cards of your opponent's deck, gives you one of their banished monsters, then, while on your field, protects your spells.

- Mimighoul Archfiend - +1 to you, discard for your opponent.

- Mimighoul Slime - Free deck special summon for a Mimighoul

- Mimighoul Armor - Mimighoul's cannot be destroyed by battle this turn

- Mimighoul Fairy - All their hand special summons can't activate their effects.

You can also have your non-XYZ Mimighouls attack directly with Giant Mimighoul's effect.

Feel free to sub in Dullahan and Ghostrick Scare to put your opponent's field on lockdown permanently.

deck_name: Gravekeeper's Deck (Legacy of the Duelist Easy Deck)
deck_description:
Found this on Steam, it is for Legacy of the Duelist Link Evolution and all cards can be acquired from the first and cheapest pack. It is great for shutting down special summons and therefore shutting down decks that rely heavily on Extra Deck monsters.

Get yourself Necrovalley on the field in tandem with Hidden Temples of Necrovalley and Gravekeeper's Spiritualist to complete the lockdown. 
Gravekeeper's Commandant can search Necrovalley.
Gravekeeper's Nobleman and Recruiter can search for Commandant.
Gravekeeper's Spy can search Nobleman or recruiter (but not Commandant).
Gravekeeper's Spiritualist can perform the Fusion for Supernaturalist.
You can further protect Necrovalley with Gravekeeper's Shaman and Necrovalley Temple.

Trying it and comparing this to clown-mitsu

honestly, you could replace the wicked avatar and pot of procras for vanity fiend. Just that this deck is more like a game ender while vanity fiend ver is more control

So sit back, theorytest, and enjoy the shock on your friends/opponents faces when you pull this awesome dragon out of nowhere in the middle of your danger noodles and goofballs.

deck_excerpt: This Blue-Eyes deck is built from 3 copies of the Blue-Eyes White Destiny Structure Deck and focuses on consistently summoning powerful Blue
deck_description: This Blue-Eyes deck is built from 3 copies of the Blue-Eyes White Destiny Structure Deck and focuses on consistently summoning powerful Blue-Eyes monsters while controlling the game with Synchro, Fusion, and disruption effects. Maiden of White, Sage with Eyes of Blue, and Neo Kaiser Sea Horse help quickly access Blue-Eyes White Dragon and set up plays for Blue-Eyes Spirit Dragon and Blue-Eyes Ultimate Spirit Dragon. Ultimate Fusion provides strong offensive and defensive options by summoning powerful Fusion monsters while recycling resources. The deck can establish a strong board going first with Spirit Dragon and hand traps, or break through opponent boards going second using Blue-Eyes Jet Dragon, Ultimate Fusion, and high-ATK dragons. This build is beginner-friendly, budget-friendly, and serves as a strong foundation for future Blue-Eyes upgrades.

1x Synchro World AFD target if you get 10 counters you can revive any Synchro monster.

deck_excerpt: It turns out that searching a in-engine dimensional fissure counters most of the meta!
deck_description:
Branded has recieved a ton of new direct and indirect support over the past year, so now it's time to revisit an unusual but vastly more powerful version of Branded - Synchro Branded! It turns out that searching a in-engine dimensional fissure counters most of the meta!

Despite it's fusion roots, Branded has leaned into the more "synchro" side of it's gameplan, especially with Fallen of the White Dragon's ability to turbo out level 8 synchro monsters. So I thought, instead of halfway being a synchro deck, why not fully embrace the power of white cards? 

With that in mind, Branded's new win condition (on top of it's usual interruptions) is to turbo out Branded Etude (banishes every monster your opponent uses as material) while spamming Bystials. With the right cards, you can also summon Bystial Dis Pater! Branded Etude specifically hard-counters Kewl Tunes, one of the decks to beat in this format. 

Here is the main combo with Starliege Seyfert and a light/dark monster in hand: 

1. Normal summon Seyfert. Activate Seyfert's effect sending itself to search for Fallen of the White Dragon. 

2. Activate Fallen of the White Dragon's effect to send Albion from the Extra Deck to summon himself. 

3. Activate Fallen of the White's Dragon's on-summon effect to summon Incredible Ecclesia from deck. 

4. Activate Incredible Ecclesia's effect to summon The Golden Swordsoul from deck. 

5. Synchro summon Ecclesia and the Black Dragon using Fallen of the White Dragon + The Golden Swordsoul. 

6. Activate Ecclesia and the Black Dragon's effect to summon Quem from deck. 

7. Activate in CL1 The Golden Swordsoul to summon a spellcaster from the GY with the same ATK/DEF (to summon Cartesia) and CL2 Quem to send Cartesia from deck to GY.

8. Activate Cartesia's effect to fusion summon Granguignol using herself + light/dark in hand. 

9. Activate in CL1 Granguignol's effect to send Bystial Lubellion from deck to GY and CL2 Quem to summon Fallen of the White Dragon from GY. 

10. Synchro summon Psy-Frame Omega using Quem + Fallen of the White Dragon. 

11. Activate Psy-Frame Omega's effect to banish a card from your opponent's hand. 

12. Banish Starliege Seyfert to activate it's effect to return Bystial Lubellion to hand from GY.

13. Activate Bystial Lubellion to search for Bystial Magnamhut from deck. 

14. Activate Magnamhut's effect to summon itself by banishing a light/dark from your GY (most likely Fallen of the White Dragon). 

15. Activate Magnamhut's effect to search a dragon during the End Phase.

16. Summon Bystial Lubellion from GY by tributing Magnamhut. 

17.  Activate Bystial Lubellion's effect to place Branded Etude face-up on the field. 

18. Go to End Phase. Ecclesia and the Black Dragon returns to the field. Albion the Branded Dragon sets from deck The Fallen and the Virtuous. Cartesia returns to hand. Incredible Ecclesia returns to hand. Bystial Magnamhut searches for Fidraulis Harmonia. 

This combo ends with a floodgate that banishes all material your opponent uses, a handrip with Psy-Framelord Omega, a Fidraulis Harmonia, a targeted destruction with The Fallen and the Virtuous, and Ecclesia and the Black Dragon can summon Albaz to fusion summon! 

There are many more combo lines that involve two cards/three cards to achieve this board or better. Here is another guide to this deck: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LERYMMYbk1A 

GLHF Duelists!

I love bug combo piles. Beetroopers got me into the game. It was the first deck I built in the TCG and they've gotten some honestly solid support with the new Battlewasp stuff and the Ragnaraika engine.
It's a hard deck to build because the starters typically all have deficiencies. Maybe I'll make a video about this deck at some point.


I'm not really coping. I know it's not that good. Can't play under charmies/maxx "c" for at all.

 

Beetroopers typically only lock while they're on field.
Battlewasps and Armed Rebeellion lock you to bugs for the rest of the turn.

Ragnaraika Evil Seed is a plant, and 3/5 of the links are plant/reptile. Be mindful of this when comboing. Typically if you lead Ragnaraika then go into something else it'll work out alright.


Insect invitation isn't a good card but I like it so I play it. 
Banishing Reso can dump Gokipole which searches Retal, If retal goes from field to grave you can search Maxx "C"
Warning: Don't activate the on summon effect of Sachi, it messes up the combo line. You don't want her to be a tuner so you can summon Diabolantis off of her and Sting


Side Deck: This is just other card options I don't always use.

Interrupted Kaiju Slumber: A solid choice as a breaker, but with how accessible Kaiju's are during your combo (Every Resonance Insect sent from field to grave can grab a Gadarla or Kumongous) I don't think it's necessary. If anything just add more Kaiju copies.

Seraphim Papillion: I go back and forth on Traptrix Atypus or Seraphim Papillion. Seraphim can summon back Sting or Scale bomber (Tribute another bug for a targeted negate or tribute any bug to pop a monster that activated an effect). I think Atypus is a stronger card. It's just preference

BW - Sting the Poison: You can play 3 Sting, but I don't love it as a starter since still needs another body to dump after grabbing rapier. 

Dragonbite: In a deck full of 2 card combos, and discards to special summon, why add a starter that uses your normal and requires a target to already be in hand. I used to play it. It's been powercrept.

Super Poly + Insect Invitation + Absolute Hercules: This is bad, but really funny.

Ultimate Great Insect + Bio Insect Armor: Since destruction effects are more of what this deck can actually put onto an endboard, this doesn't add very much. Not a bad card by any means. Bio Insect Armor is easily grabbable off of Reso, so if you like this feel free to go for it.

deck_excerpt: "Gem-Knights" Working in tandem with other Fusion, and Normal Monster Spells to create a smooth experience.
deck_description: "Gem-" Archtype, using other Spells that aid your Normal Monsters, and help you unleash the full power of your Fusion Monsters allow you to have lots of fun destroying your enemies with the shiniest monsters in any Extra Deck!

The goal here is just to extra deck rip as much as possible. You have 3 engines that can ED rip. Obviously Kash does this very easily, and the others don't. In theory, the ideal scenario for this deck lets you resolve Unicorn, Xeno, and Charm (which is twice per turn). You can rip 4 before they get a turn. Then a 5th immediately once their main phase starts. 
You need to high roll pretty hard on ghouls or hands to make a xeno or a charm happen. 
You can get charm easily, but making it do something useful for you is harder. You might want to play more generic links to get rid of crap you don't want.
Silhouhatte can theoretically bridge to Jauzah if you steal something with charm.
Flipping Mimighouls up on turn 1 allows you to use Thrust/Talent AND triggers Unicorn/Fenrir. If you manage to make a ripple bird and flip something up then you can thrust into Feather Storm. It's crazy strong. Zeus/Fucho/Big Eye are not monsters I summon often. Feel free to swap them for whatever.

Also, 3/6 main deck hecahand monsters are level 7. You can normal summon them with Birth. Any of them + a kashtira monster makes Shangri ira. Getting 3 level 7s for Arise-heart is pretty common! 

Jauzah + any opponent's monster makes TZV

I've been messing around with the ratios a bunch. Hecahands are tough because the ones that act like handtraps don't do anything aside from summoning themselves. The only one that does do something is Godos, but you don't want to play too many copies of him because he can't combo (His steal effect is opponent's turn only)
