deck_excerpt: A grindy DARK Fiend control deck built around Yubel.
deck_description: This is a Yubel control-combo deck that wins on its own terms: rather than removing the opponent's monsters, it forces them to attack into its 0 ATK/DEF Yubel monsters under Nightmare Pain and reflects the battle damage back — escalating through the line of Spirit of Yubel → Yubel → Yubel - Terror Incarnate → Yubel - The Ultimate Nightmare, the last of which banishes and burns twice. Consistency comes from the Sacred Beast package (Dark Beckoning Beast + Opening of the Spirit Gates), which doubles as a one-card starter and grants an extra Normal Summon to deploy Samsara D Lotus, the deck's premier engine that pitches Yubel monsters straight from the deck. The Fiendsmith splash (Engraver, Tract, and the Requiem/Sequence/Lacrima/Desirae line) supplies LIGHT-Fiend value, fusion power, and graveyard recursion, while a light Unchained sub-engine (Soul of Sharvara → Soul Lord of Yama → Soul of Rage) plus I:P Masquerena into S:P Little Knight add the interactive removal the pure archetype lacks. Bystial Dragons (Magnamhut, Saronir) banish from either graveyard for disruption and extension, and the Buio / Mutiny in the Sky package rounds out the Fiend Fusion toolbox. End boards lean on Phantom of Yubel's negation and relentless Nightmare Pain pressure; going second, the deck pivots into Super Polymerization and Yubel - The Loving Defender Forever for explosive burn. Rewards patient, reactive play and reading the opponent over raw tempo.

deck_excerpt: By utilizing "Stinger the Electric Expeller," Materiactor is able to bridge into the Azamina combo through Rciela.
deck_description:
By utilizing "Stinger the Electric Expeller," Materiactor is able to bridge into the Azamina combo through Rciela.

1. Begin with Gigadra or Exaraptor to add or special summon Stinger.

2. Use Stinger and either starter to XYZ into Materiactor Exagard.

3. Activate Exagard's effect and detach Stinger to special summon Zeptwing from deck.

4. Use Zeptwing's effect on summon to first add Materiactor Meltdown and then use it's second effect to special summon Stinger from grave. (Note: can also search Materiactor Critical as well, but Meltdown is preferred if you don't have another monster in hand. It's personal preference for your endboard.)

5. (If you don't have a monster in hand, activate Materiactor Meltdown to excavate 6 from deck and add a Materiactor monster from deck to hand.)

6. Synchro summon Rciela using Zeptwing and Stinger. Use Rciela's effect to send Meltdown to the GY and add Elzette to hand from deck.

7. Reveal and special summon Elzette.

8. Synchro summon Diabell, Queen of the White Forest.

9. Chain 1-> Elzette effect in GY to add Deception of the Sinful Spoils to hand; Chain 2-> Diabell effect to add Meltdown back to hand.

10. Activate Deception of the SS. Tribute a monster in hand (which may have been search by Meltdown in step 5) to add Hallowed Azamina from deck to hand.

11. Activate Hallowed Azamina to summon Rcielago by sending Deception of the SS to GY.

12. Rcielago effect to add Sinful Spoils of the White Forest

Note: While you will be fusing away Diabell Queen of the WF next, another option is simply stopping here and using "SS of the WF" as cost for Diabell's QE to summon a tuner from the Extra Deck. It depends on your endboard preference.

13. Activate Hallowed Azamina in GY to shuffle back Elzette and add itself to hand.

14. Activate Hallowed Azimina to special summon Azamina Ilia Silva and chain Sinful Spoils of the White Forest to this activation. Use Sinful Spoils of the White Forest to Fusion summon Saint Azamina using Diabell Queen of the WF and Rcielago.

15. Activate Saint Azamina's effect to special summon Azamina Moa Regina (going first) or Azamina Sol Erysichthon (going second to pick apart opponent's board.

 

Your resulting endboard from 1 card will be:

- Materiactor Exagard (QE add material to hand to bounce an opponent's monster to hand) ( ! OR if you added Materiactor Critical, you can use Exagard's material as cost for an omni-negate)

- Azamina Ilia Silvia

- Saint Azamina

- Azamina Moa Regina

- Materiactor Meltdown or Critical

- Deception of the Sinful Spoils (resets itself during end phase)

 

Aside from this combo, the Primite engine is here due to the normal monster support.

Alternative Options: If you'd prefer, you could cut some extra deck spots to add in the Fiendsmith engine via Melomelody the Brass Djinn.

deck_excerpt: When just one FTK won’t do!
deck_description:
Gimmick Puppet is one of my favorite pet decks ever, and Fanatix being banned in TCG is literally the worst thing you could do to my soul.

Which is why I made this! This deck is designed to be able to do the Dark Strings FTK and Disaster Leo FTK with the addition of a Horus engine.

If you don't see the field spell after the first Dark Strings activation, you can alternatively rank up Fantasix into Giant Hunter, pop Cattle Scream with its effect, and set the PK Rank-Up spell to make Varudras on the opponent's turn for an omni-negate and a pop.

Orcust Crescendo is in for a Burial Goods Target + it searches or recovers your banished Gimmick Puppets. For example: send Terror Baby off Condolence Puppet, use its effect to prevent your opponent from responding to GP monster activations, use Orcust Crescendo in grave to add it back to hand to revive either Dark Strings for the FTK or Fanatix for a special summon pop if you don't get the FTK.

If you have any suggestions or questions, feel free to leave a comment! :D

deck_name: Ritual of Light and Darkness Runick (not TCG legal yet)
deck_excerpt: Ritual of Light and Darkness, Runick
deck_description:
Runick and Ritual of Light and Darkness have excellent synergy together. Skull Archfiend of Chaos can be discarded of Hugin the Runick Wings search search effect, so any runick spell + the new Skull Archfiend starts your RLD combo. In addition, Skull Archfiend's effect can recover Runick Fountain and Runick monsters from the Graveyard and Banishment, helping you maintain your resources.

Magician of Dark Chaos - Black Chaos also has great synergy because it protects Runick Fountain while allowing you to recover Runick Spells from the Graveyard. It can also be used as a bridge into Red-Eyes Dark Dragoon, giving you an omni-negate. Furthermore, it can recover The Eye of Timaeus, enabling you to summon a second Dark Magician fusion monster on turn 3.

Dark Magician, the Pharaoh's Servant and Illusion of Chaos are included because Dark Magician of Destruction can search Illusion of Chaos with its effect, them ou can then Link Summon using Hugin and Dark Magician of Destruction to make Ashlan U1000. By revealing Illusion of Chaos in your hand, you can search Black Luster Soldier – Soldier of Light and Darkness. Afterward,  you can use Illusion of Chaos's effect to search Dark Magician, the Magician of Black Magic, summon it, and activate its effect to set The Eye of Timaeus.

On the following turn, you can summon Red-Eyes Dark Dragoon by shuffling Dark Magician of Destruction from the Graveyard. You can also use Dark Magician, the Pharaoh's Servant as a target for the Mindshuffle Summon since it is a Level 7 or higher non-Extra Deck monster, allowing you to summon the new Black Luster Soldier and banish a card.

The Side Deck contains additional Main Deck and Extra Deck alternatives that I didn't inclued in the deck.

- Metamorphosis lets you cheat out Fusions, bypassing the "must be fusion summoned" clause.

- Thunder Dragon puts a light monster in your graveyard, effectively draws you 2, and hits your level 5 metamorphosis targets

- Sinister Serpent is free ritual material, tribute and discard fodder, and is a cheeky metamorphosis target for thousand-eyes

- King of the Swamp fetches you Polymerization and can fuse with Thunder Dragon, Summoned Skull, Relinquished, Spirit Reaper, and Sangan

- Tsukoyomi & Book of Moon can let you re-trigger Manju. Senju is effectively a 4th copy to fetch Relinquished. 

- Cyber-Stein lets you cheat out any fusion monster for any use-case, gives you access to Master of Oz as the strongest monster in the format.


List is kind of greedy. But it plays double duty as a beatdown and a control deck. Sideboard options are good if you go against another trap-light deck or just feel like the fusion package is bogging you down and you want a bit more consistency.

deck_description:
Category: Tournament Meta Decks (TCG)

Tournament Meta Decks are current-level Meta Decks that are capable of going up against the best of the best. These are curated lists that cannot be uploaded by regular users.

Creator: Craig Smith

Tournament: Rosemont WCQ Regional – May 30th 2026

Placement: Top 8

Based on the topping list from Genesys YCS Columbus, with a few changes.

-Only 1 Form Change, I didn't really like using this card when the more fringe Masked HERO targets aren't that impactful to begin with.

-Opted for a 3rd Dusk Crow. Furnace's lock doesn't feel as punishing when we can't run links to begin with, so I wanted to see him more often. However, Dusk Crow tends to be better to draw than Furnace IMO since it lets you set up Contrast or Atomic more easily.

-Messed with the Extra Deck more. No 2nd Anki, no Koga. Instead, I've added a Shining Neos Wingman and a Marine Dolphin.

The former is a potential pivot point if your opponent keeps you from getting to Cosmo/Nebula Neos since it's much easier to set up in exchange for a weaker reward.

Marine Dolphin is there as an option to summon off of Instant Contact, both as a potential handrip and as a way to get a third Neo-Spacian into rotation if we've already used EN Shuffle earlier to get Stratos or something. The only downside is that it conflicts with Furnace's lock, but I'd say that getting access to Cosmo is a bit better than having Contrast or Ab-Zero into Acid online, since the former basically wins the game on the spot.

deck_description: My deck for Rebecca Hawkins, themed around the gem dragons she used in the anime.

Trying to make springans with the release of the new support in MD but can be played in tcg im pretty sure. Could get costly though so if you dont wanna pay much just cut out all the branded stuff but leave branded in high spirits and at least 1 sprind in your extra deck then replace what you cut with whatever staples, non engine, handtraps you want or even a package made of another archetype like mathmech or ryzeal. Becomes pretty cheap after you cut them out. I wouldn't say this is a top tier springans list, I mainly made this with the mindset that if im gonna play such a non-meta deck like this I might as well just have fun with it and put whatever I wanted in it. I was considering running both utopic draco future over utopic future zexal but took 2 extra deck slots so I didn't. Pedor is cuttable 100% but I like pedor so I kept him in there. My main tip is just to have fun with this deck, definitely not very meta, you'll lose a lot of games in high ranks/against meta and theres a lot of better variants than this such as a more branded heavy version but personally I wanted to keep it mainly springans. If you want you can cut original fallen of albaz for another white albaz but the OG albaz being there lets you be able to fuse away a bunch of monsters on your opponent's turns by summoning it with the synchro ecclesia. Having another white albaz just gives you higher odds of doing what it does normally. Note if you do cut original albaz for white albaz, cut the synchro and take out a sprind or keep the second sprind if you'd like it for some other reason. Ty to springans discord server for the help and suggestions like the inclusion of original albaz.

deck_excerpt: Board Breakers and a consistent OTK line
deck_description:
This is the deck I took to German Nationals with a few changes with new knowlege from that tournament. It felt incredibly competent against most top tier decks, as board breakers feel very rewarding right now.

The version I played at nats was without Radiant Typhoon Vision  and The Fallen & The Virtuous, but instead droll, scatter fusions and lightning storms. Droll and storm came up, but had the lowest impact of my non-engine. Scatter Fusion itself is now fully cut, even though they are 1-card starters- against so many decks playing Fallen & the Virtuous right now, it did not resolve often enough and was completly dead the few times I went 1st.

 

With the 6th card going second I very consistently drew enough engine to OTK and enough board breakers to secure board presence. Too much engine was bearable, as Gem-Knights can chew through a lot.

Visions proved themselves incredibly well, as drawing more breakers is great and even allows omitting a going-first gameplan somewhat. At nats I sided a Infernal Flame Banshee  -> Nemeses Flag -> Nemeses Corridor  -> Thunder Dragon Colossus line, which is powerfull if it resolves, but simply contained too many chokepoints to ever do so.

Right now, I'm considering changing or even dropping Fallen targets in the extra deck. Synchro Ecclesia is great as we do play a level 8 fusion to use her effect to spin a card, but I'm struggling to see the utility of the 2nd one (Albion atm), as I'm not planning on seeing that end phase if I OTK. It's another Super-Poly target at best. I think Forbidden Crown is a great side deck option for going first, as you can simply crown your own monster, live that turn and go pseudo 2nd right after. It's hella expensive though.

Few tidbits at the end, I cut the Gem-Knight Hollowcore to 1, as the second never came up. MST for the vision is too weak, so it went to the side. And no Scatter Fusions, as I can search Brilliant Fusion and don't play enough Gems in the main to resolve two.

Lots of words for someone who only went 5-4, but I felt 3 of those losses were mostly attributed to the pilot lacking matchup knowlege.

deck_excerpt: The new Branded support propels Dracotail to it's ultimate form and the strongest deck in Masterduel!
deck_description:
The new Branded support cards from the latest selection pack "Order of the New World" propels Dracotail to it's ultimate form! Arguably, this ties Dracotail with Kewl Tune as the strongest strategy in Masterduel! 

On top of Dracotail's solid playstyle of smashing through boards while plussing with it's fusion materials, it now has Fallen of the White Dragon's ability to access Branded cards while synchro summoning!

The Branded card that is used most often is the incredibly powerful and versatile "The Fallen & The Virtuous," which can be used to destroy a card on either field while sending an Albaz fusion for cost! This allows you to dodge targeting effects like Imperm/Veiler by destroying your own monster, allowing them to go through! You can also reborn a monster from either graveyard with it's secondary effect, so the card can be an extender, interruption and a playmaker!

Ecclesia and the Dark Dragon give Dracotail an immensely powerful grindgame, granting access to Cartesia so you can start your Dracotail plays with quick effect fusion summoning. She can also summon her BF Albaz so you can fuse away your opponent's board, especially with the new Dragon that Devours the Dogma! Ecclesia and the Dark Dragon can also effectly remove problematic cards from the field as well by recycling herself and another level 8 fusion monster. 

Fallen of the White Dragon, The Fallen & The Virtuous and Ecclesia and the Dark Dragon grant Dracotail an immense boost in power, grindgame and plays! The whole time, the deck is pretty much is immune to Droll, Nibiru, Maxx "C," and the Mulcharmies (if you play properly)! 

Enjoy playing the strongest deck of the format, and the best fusion strategy of all time fellow fusionist!

Want to see the deck in action? Scroll to the very bottom for the Youtube video!

GLHF Duelists!

deck_name: 2 color goodstuff wtf am i cooking

deck_description:
This deck focuses on unleashing an onslaught of traps, then pushing for game with Labrynth Archfiend. Archfiend Playtime can special summon Labrynth Archfiend from your hand or deck. Labrynth Archfiend can special summon itself from your hand if a trap is activated. Call of the Archfiend can special summon Labrynth Archfiend from your graveyard by discarding a fiend. Archfiend's Usurpation can search for any of your Archfiend traps, including the floodgate Simul Archfiends, which can prevent your opponent from special summoning a Ritual, Fusion, Synchro, Xyz, or Link monster if they already control a monster with one of the aforementioned card types.

Supporting this strategy is a small fiend engine consisting of Fiendish Rhino Warrior, Diabolica the Draconique General, and the Terrifying Toddler of Torment. If Fiendish Rhino Warrior is sent to your graveyard, then you can send a fiend from your deck to the graveyard. If you send the Toddler of Torment from your deck to your graveyard, then you special summon it in face-down defense position. If the Toddler of Torment is flipped face-up, then you can send another fiend from your deck to your graveyard. If you send Diabolica from your deck to your graveyard with a card effect, then you can add a fiend, except Diabolica, from your graveyard to your hand. Diabolica also has another great effect that allows you to special summon her from your hand or graveyard if a fiend was sent to your graveyard with a card effect.

A basic resource loop you can do is:

1. Send Fiendish Rhino Warrior to your graveyard.

2. Activate Fiendish Rhino Warrior's effect to send Diabolica the Draconique General from your deck to your graveyard.

3. Activate Diabolica's effect to add Fiendish Rhino Warrior back to your hand.

This gives you a spare fiend monster in your hand to use as discard fodder. Terrifying Toddler of Torment can start this loop with its flip effect.

Metamorphosis theory deck. The idea of using Lacrima to send metamorphosis to the graveyard and use Dark Magician/Chaos Ritual to return it to the hand. Starter: Witchcrafter Creation / Spenta, The Magistus Sealer

The idea of using Visas Amritara to add Realm Energy to access Veda Kalanta and using the unreleased synchro monster Glitch Cluch Nullgainer to approach Veda Kalarcanum and special summon an additional body of Mannadium Meek.

The idea of using level up system of Mannadium Meek to xyz into Tellarknight Constellar Cadeus to access Tellarknight Cygnian. Using Cygnian to search Constellar Caduceus and using Cygnian effect to level up to 5 and approach Number 33. After that, using Visas Amritara and Vicious Astraloud to special summon Phantasmal Lord Ultimitl Bishbaalkin. Don't use its effect right away, if you figure it out properly, there should be 2 left away body of Cross-Sheep and any monster to link into Geonator Transveser. Make sure Geonator Transveser point to Phantasmal Lord. If you figure it out correctly, your field will have 3 body of Geonator, Phantasmal Lord and Number 33. Use the effect of Phantasmal Lord to summon token in link marker of Geonator. BAM!

Starter: 2 card combo Mannadium Riumheart + Abscission/any mannadium monster

The Idea of using Morphtronic Telefon to loop summoning Saryuja Skull Dread to search for 4 DIVINE monsters. Fiendsmith and Horus engine were used to thin the deck as much as possible. Fiendsmith can be used to access Aerial Eater to send Skull Archfiend of Chaos, accessing the Chaos Ritual engine. The Dark Magician engine was used to control the top-deck card. 
After all, this deck is hilarious to surprise friends. It doesn't have any competitiveness. Therefore, if you want to impress, use it.

deck_excerpt: The triumphant return of the Beastly Alliance!
deck_description: The Beastly Alliance enters the arena once more to fight and entertain, in its strongest form to date.

Reminiscing on the good old days let me try my old petdeck again and realise that they have long gone past their golden(hair) days. This deck is in desperate need of that wind ship, alongside new support (like a spell card that actively does something). In the interim, you can have this! 
This deck has many issues and is very susceptible to proper disruption (the Dominus cards), but most people dont know how to play against it, so you have an inherent advantage. Lemme break it down.

MAIN ENGINE:

3x Goldenhair, the Newest Plunder Patroll : Your main combo starter, alongside Whitebeard. Can summon itself from hand to trigger your Plunders, can revive itself while triggering your Plunders (or dumping relevant cards), and is your best way to get to Jord, your best starter. Can get to Blackbeard by itself on normal summon. Of course you run 3.

3x Whitebeard, the Plunder Patroll Helm : That boi. the Man. The other main combo starter AND extender. He's HIM. On discard is either summoning Goldenhair to get to Jord, Blackeyes to get to Jord, Bluebeard if you have the other 2 already, and Redbeard on your opponent's turn (or any other Plunder that you want to turn into a ship with Blackbeard). He can also turn into a ship. Super versatile, of course you run 3.

3x Blackeyes, the Plunder Patroll Seaguide : A pirate's best friend and your best extender. It can retrieve any plunder monster, even the ships, because it summons regardless of whether it goes back to hand. Best used as material for Jord, then returned to hand by Jord. With it's effect your best options to retrieve are either Whitebeard (best case scenario), or Bluebeard (for extender purposes). Its secondary effect isnt used much, but bouncing your Plunder monsters that are on ships that have already resolved their effects is a good way to maintain cannon fodder for the ships. Run 3.

3x Bluebeard, the Plunder Patroll Shipwright : He's everyone's best friend. On normal can trigger the rest of the plunders in your hand, while drawing a card. And if a plunder is on field, can summon himself, but most importantly, the only relevant Plunder that doesnt straight up xenolock you. Just a solid extender in all scenarios, run 3.

1x Redbeard, the Plunder Patroll Matey : This one is fine. Its not really an extender, and not a starter either, but it has its uses. It can equip itself to any ship that has no crew on it, which comes up more than you think (for Lys in particular). Can also summon out any ship on your opponent's turn. You need as many Plunder names as possible, but anymore copies of this card feels incredibly bricky. One copy is enough.

1x Plunder Patroll Shipyarrrd : Obvious addition. Can search whichever Plunder you need exactly at the moment, and can also put relevant cards in the GY (Goldenhair and Mutiny), or just trigger your Plunders by itself. Also the means by which you can beat over larger ATK values. Can also retrieve itself from GY, which is why youre only running a single copy, though running more is kinda debatable. 

1x Plunder Patroll Booty : The best trap card for Plunder. It can revive Blackbeard, Whitebeard, or Redbeard, while getting the relevant attribute needed. Can also just retrieve spent ships from the GY if you're running low. Mainly needed because you need a lot of Plunder names. Plunder Patroll Parrrty is a debatable subtitute, due to Mutiny being your preferred way to summon Lys, and its GY effect can equip to Lys when summoned that way, or on Moerk when hard summoned.

3x Dipsea Fiend : Your piece of non-archetypal support CLEARLY meant to support Plunders. Can potentailly start full combo with the right hand, forces mulcharmies, stops Infinite Impermanence , locks the zones for Elfnote, forcing a normal summon, and so much more. It can allow you to get to whichever ship you want due to changing its own attribute, and you dont have to worry about Fiendsmith's Requiem because its attribute changes back in the end phase. You want to see it often, so run 3.

3x Buio the Dawn's Light : Godbless this card. Its my answer to the Fiendsmith problem that Jord causes. Its an extender on a normal summoned Plunder to get to Blackbeard, while getting Mutiny. Can also get Mutiny on normal summon due to Almiraj. Mutiny is needed, so run 3.

1x Mutiny in the Sky : Let me paint a picture. Jord gets released, great support. Finally Earth ship. It gives your opponent a token of any attribute allowing you to access any ship on turn 1. Excellent! Lets get your best ship, Lys, while setting up a Blackbeard and fodder in hand as well. Life is good. You end your turn. Whoops, you fool, you troglodyte, you absolute neanderthal. You allowed your opponent to get to Fiendsmith Requiem without needing to summon a single monster. Life is no longer Daijoubou. This is where Mutiny comes in (also its on theme, haha get it? Mutiny? It's like a pirate ship nevermind just continue). It can summon your Lys without giving your opponent full Fiendsmith combo. It can also be discarded by Goldenhair, as it can recur itself by sending a fiend to GY, triggering the Plunders. Can also retrieve all your ships to summon Luce, which can dump any Plunder thats needed (mainly Goldenhair). A godsend to the Fiendsmith dilemma, thank you Mr. Konami.

SUB-ENGINE:

1x Fallen of the White Dragon : The sub-engine of choice for this brew. "But Ajax, its SS effect locks you out of Blackbeard and Moerk, its not sustainable" I hear you saying in your head already. Worry not, Plunder doesn't need its normal summon, so you can go ahead and summon him to get to Incredible Ecclesia. From there you can either Go into Synchro Ecclesia, which can summon back Incredible, so that you have 2 methods to get to Fallen of Albaz on your opponent's turn, or just a way to get to Jord again. Pretty solid from my testings, much better than other engines I've tried. But you don't want it clogging up your hand, or being drawn off of Blackbeard, since we need as much plunder names as possible, so 1 copy is fine. You can run more if you prefer, I guess. 

1x Incredible Ecclesia, the Virtuous : What more can be said that hasn't been said above? Technically a better draw that White Albaz, because it can summon itself without locking you, and gets to White Albaz regardless, or be used offensively to get to Regular Albaz to fusion for Mirrorjade or Dragon that Devours the Dogma. All Plunders are generic, so can also be used as a way to Jord, or even Bran if necessary. I'm debating running more copies, but for now, 1 is fine.

1x Fallen of Albaz : Your offensive option off of the Ecclesia cards (or Fallen & Virtuous if you have an Ecclesia in GY). MIrrorjade takes care of extra deck monsters, and Dragon that Devours takes care of LIGHT/Dark monsters (pretty common), and any other effect monster. One other great thing about this Albaz engine is that it chases out interaction, that would normally end your turn if it stopped your Plunder cards. Single copy is fine.

2x The Fallen & The Virtuous : Great generic removal tool, which can double of a Monster Reborn in a pinch. You aren't using White Albaz's first effect often enough to justify 3, but more than one is good, since Albion (or Dogma) can be sent to the GY and can search it again. Just.... be careful which one you send, because it can screw with your fusion targets for regular Albaz. 2 is fine, but you can run 3 of you wish.

NON-ENGINE:

3x Mulcharmy Fuwalos : Does this really need to be justified? The more the merrier. Obvious choice.

3x Ash Blossom & Joyous Spring : We're unable to play any of the Dominus cards, since we use all attributes (except WIND 🥲) so Ash Blossom just makes sense. Very versatile, almost always useful. Obvious choice.

3x Forbidden Droplet : Spell Speed 4 negation (situational), while triggering your Plunders. Can also send Buio to get Mutiny, or send Mutiny to use it to trigger Plunders on another chain, or send equips from ships that have already used its effect. Genuinely one of your best non-engines. Obvious choice.

1x Called by the Grave : Your sole defense againt the possible worst thing to happen to a Plunder deck. Droll & Lock Bird . Ash Blossom also screws you over, because Blackbeard also draws a card. And because all the ships also add a card, it can be stopped by Ash, and Droll prevents you from keeping fodder in hand. Man, this deck is in desperate need of support. Anyway, this card is needed for obvious reasons.

1x Triple Tactics Talent : This is a really good card, does it's inclusion really need to be justified? Obvious choice.

1x Foolish Burial : The main sends off of this is Buio, to get to Mutiny early, or Goldenhair to get a free way to discard and a body on board. If you don't have Blakeyes in GY at the time of summoning Jord, you can send that so you can get it back to hand to continue extending. Pretty good, run the 1 copy.

1x Terraforming : This spot used to be Emblem of the Plunder Patroll due to it also being able to bypass the Fiendsmith dilemma (Ecclesia is a LIGHT monster), but in testing, every time I drew that card it either felt unnecessary as it really was just an extra card, or felt awful and I wished I just had a way to proper Plunder combo. Due to that, I realised I wanted more consistency, and that consistency was in the Shipyarrrd, so with 1 slot left to keep it at 40 cards, Terraforming felt like the best option. "Why not just run extra copies of Shipyarrrd?" I hear you think to yourself out loud. To that I say... Shut up! Ok yeah, you can do that, running multiple Shipyarrrds isn't a bad idea if you want. Anyway, Terraforming is still good, run the 1.

EXTRA DECK:

3x Blackbeard, the Plunder Patroll Captain : The heart and soul of the deck. Enables any and everything. Not an exagerration to say the entire deck revolves around this card. Get to any ship at quick effect speed, and draws a card to keep up card advantage. Can be used to dodge targetting as well. Just a solid card. Run 3, you're gonna be going through them multiple times.

1x Plunder Patrollship Jord : Despite Blackbeard being the heart and soul, you're actually summoning this first. It retrieves any Plunder from th GY and places itself in the pendulum. In the pendulum zone, returns to the extra deck and creates 2 tokens on both fields of any attribute, making it so that you can gain access to any ship you want! Solves the major problem of Plunder's first turn, and also cuts off Imperm usage. Fiendsmith dilemma still exists though, but thats what Mutiny is for, so you can make the tokens DARK instead to get to Moerk (or FIRE against a deck you know relies on spell/trap). Its on field effect is also pretty nuts, so summoning it off Blackbeard is pretty crazy, since it adds a Plunder to hand then can SS Whitebeard and Redbeard afterwards, allowing another ship to be summoned with definite fodder in hand. Don't tell Lys, but Jord is actually the best ship.

2x Plunder Patrollship Lys : THE BEST SHIP. Its the (much needed) Monster negation of the deck, and can also summon Plunders back from the spell/trap or from hand. So if you used Blackbeard to summon it, after you negate, you can just summon back Blackbeard to get into another ship. Also, on negation (AND DESTRUCTION IT NEEDS TO DESTROY TO SEARCH) it can get any Plunder card from deck. You can get Whitebeard, which is the objectively best discard, or Shipyarrd if you wanna set up the next turn. Extra deck space is tight, so only 2.

2x Plunder Patrollship Moerk : THE (2nd) BEST SHIP. Monster banisher of the deck (dodges destruction prevention) so its the most offensively geared ship in your arsenal, and can search any Plunder spell/trap (the argument to run Parrrty instead of Booty). Plus, one very underrated effect that most people don't pick up on. Hard summoning it grants destruction protection, which definitely comes up. Extra deck space is tight so only 2.

1x Plunder Patrollship Brann : THE SHIP. Your answer to spell/trap related issues, as it banishes the spell/trap (dodges destruction prevention) and can search any Plunder monster afterwards. Its also the highest ATK of your ships, and it pumps all fiends by 500 ATK, so its the most Aggressive of your ships. The banishing effect doesnt come up as often, and extra deck space is tight so unfortunately only 1 copy.

1x Salamangreat Almiraj : The only purpose in the deck is to send Goldenhair, Bluebeard, or Buio to the GY. It can help start combos in a pinch, get used as material for Blackbeard. That's it lol. 1 of.

1x Luce the Dusk's Dark : Summon off of Mutiny to retrieve your spent ships that are stranded in the GY. Plus its additional destruction and destruction prevention. Good card, 1 of.

1x Ecclesia and the Dark Dragon : Your main summon off of White Albaz and Incredible Ecclesia, since it sets of Regular Albaz disruption on your opponent's turn. Can also return Lys to the extra deck for another piece of removal. Very good and versatile.

1x Albion the Branded Dragon : Your main send off of White Albaz if you can afford to do it. If youve already set up Blackbeard, you can go ahead and use its effect as you're really only going for the lvl 8 fusions and synchros anyway. Unless you want to go for Moerk first. Do that beforehand. Regardless, also comes up as an Albaz fusion target. After you do, you can just go into Mirrorjade, or actually choose not to and save Mirrorjade for later disruption.

1x Mirrorjade the Iceblade Dragon : Ah Mirrorjade. Good ole Mirrorjade. Nothing beats Mirrorjade. Non-targetting banishing, while dumping Albion (or Dogma) to later get Fallen & Virtuous. And if they somehow destroy it, it blows up the opponent's field in the End Phase. very good stuff. 

1x The Dragon that Devours the Dogma : The best fusion target for Albaz. And its a Towers if Ecclesia is in the GY. PLUS it also shuffles cards back into the deck from GY/banishment, which can be used both offensively or for recursion needs. Solid card.

The Side Deck can be whatever you wish to deal with the meta accordingly. 
AND THATS THE DECK. Try it, it's fun. Disclaimer though, this deck is HARD. Any small misstep and you've lost the game. Make sure you know when and how youre using the effects of each card, so that you dont brick yourself and end your own turn. But I can confirm, when the deck works, its so much fun. Now join me and harass Konami until they give this deck its much needed legacy support. Happy sailing!

I got a Royal Finish copy of Tierra, Source of Destruction and thought it would be fun to try and build a deck around it. It is very hard to summon, but the payoff is quite strong, shuffling EVERYTHING from both players' fields, hands and GYs back into the deck.

The goal of the deck is to generate 10+ different cards among your hand and field, while going through Fiendsmith's Tract to search Tierra, Source of Destruction. Ostinato is probably the best starter for this, but you can also make it work with Ame no Habakiri no Mitsurugi and/or Pre-Preparation of Rites. After shuffling everything back, the only card you have to worry is the one your opponent draws for turn, so you can make use of Serpentine Princess, summoning Metal Illusionist to setup Red-Eyes Black Fullmetal Dragon on your opponent's turn.

When you get back to your turn, you can go into Fiendsmith combo as a follow-up play, while also having an omni-negate on your board.

It is a gimmicky deck, but at least it mostly uses "real" cards so you have alternative game plans when you get interrupted and can't summon Tierra. Hope you enjoy it, let me know your thoughts!

The target you want varies, this deck is very non-deterministic with no one card combos, but its endboard often includes your choice from the several flip floodgates included, or simply cheating out a fusion boss monster thanks to summoner of illusions.
